description:

description:
Beautiful peaceful healthy home

The space
Home is fun healthy.
Your Night’s Sleep: 100% cotton sheets and a fluffy down comforter.
Just pack your favorite comfy shoes and bring your empty suitcases to fill.

The Blue room has a twin bed built on the legs of a piano from the 1800's. There is a dresser and a nice size closet for all your clothes. Hairdryer, bedding and towels are provided in your room. The room is sound proof, no street noise, perfect for jet-lag recovery.
I feel that if my guests have a good nights sleep, they are full of energy for their adventures, then my guests are happy.

The Common Space: approximately 500 square feet, features an Eat-in kitchen, living room and bathroom, to share. There is never more than 2 guests here at a time, this way there is enough space for each of us to get lost in, if we need too.

WIFi, and Netflix,

The home front is a work of art in constant change.


Please book online, because I cannot hold your requested days...
Also I can only host singles...

Guest access
Kitchen, living room, just like home.

During your stay
As roommates, fun, relax

Other things to note
We have the best restaurants in the neighborhood, so your tummy will be happy.

listing_name: Perfect for Your Parents, With Garden & Patio
description:
Parents/grandparents coming to town or are you just here for work or play? You'll love this Victorian home. You will enjoy your own spacious bedroom and bathroom, with all the privacy you need. Garden access. Central air conditioning. Hi-def 48-inch smart TV. High-speed internet. King-size bed. (Also a sofa bed if you don't want to share.)

We try to greet you upon check-in.

NYC short-term rental. registration #: OSE-STRREG-0000008.

The space
Very spacious, at 1,000 sq ft: California King-size bed, en suite bathroom with shower (no tub). Bar area has cooktop, microwave, fridge/freezer, sink, coffeepot, teapot as well as coffee, tea and essentials, and spacious sitting area perfect for curling up with a book or TV show Full kitchen is shared upstairs. Also, there are not 8 rooms! (as Airbnb's AI insists).

Guest access
You may access all spaces within the apartment. Private entrance and space. Shared space for washer/dryer in building laundry room. Garden access, with large patio and table.

During your stay
We'll be available throughout your stay. If you need anything, please text or call us and we'll get right on it.

Other things to note
Contact host w/questions regarding requirements of NYC short-term rental law.

description:
Easy access to all downtown has to offer! One of the least expensive stays in all of Manhattan. East Village is known for being trendy, artsy, and home to diverse food, clubs, dives, street art, and the cultural heart of New York City.

Apartment is in back of building and relatively quiet. Fast Wifi. Desk. Shared kitchen. Good fit for internship, relocating, or visit to NYC.

Subways F, L, N, R, W, 6 within walking distance. Bus on corner. Citi Bike Station & laundry mat 1 block away.

The space
Private room with comfortable queen size memory foam mattress in 8 x 10 space. Has storage & desk with fan & air conditioner during warmer months. Heat pipe runs throughout apartment during colder months. Guests from all over the world have stayed here over the years. You will share the kitchen & bathroom with 2 other people. 3 rooms and 3 people total share the apartment including you.

Best for budget travelers looking for a safe, clean, convenient home base to experience NYC. Again it is in a three bedroom BASEMENT apartment. IMPORTANT NOTE: you must enter through building courtyard to get to apartment - you will pass recycling & garbage stored in the courtyard. It is taken out three times a week so at times things pile up there. The apartment itself is quite cozy, and each room is newly renovated with wood floors and memory foam mattresses.

Experienced Host - ask me any questions you may have and read some of the reviews. If you have any questions or concerns before or during your stay please communicate! For travelers wanting to be in an awesome neighborhood for an affordable price, this is one of the best options out there.
--------------------------------

Guest access
- Fast Internet WiFi
- Roku TV with Streaming Netflix
- Sheets/Blankets and towels included
- Kitchen & Fridge for your use
- Bathroom
- Ironing board
- Hair dryer
- 24 hour Grocery Store 1 block away
- Laundry 2 blocks away
- USB Plugs and Converter

During your stay
Depending on your arrival time you will likely self-check in and pickup keys from lock-box. During your stay I am available to answer questions and provide information so you can enjoy the city. Mostly each guest does their own thing, and sometimes meet great traveling friends!

Other things to note
Check-In/Out:

We will coordinate around both of our schedules. Check-in default is after 2pm. If you arrive the day a guest is leaving you may need to wait until the evening for the room to be ready.

Please be respectful of other people in the apartment. So keep volume down 10pm-10am. Most travelers are out all day and only sleep at the apartment, but others have work to do in the city.

Enjoy!

description:
Super cute 1 bedroom apartment in a 100 year old, historical Brooklyn brownstone. We are central in Brooklyn and right across the street from the subway. Lots of Cafes and bars nearby. 10 minutes to lower Manhattan and SOHO, 30 minutes to Times Square.

The space
If you're traveling to New York City don’t settle for a cramped hotel room. Stay in an Historic Brownstone and get a true taste of living as a New Yorker.

This 1 bedroom apartment with attached den, is best suited for 2 guests, but can sleep 4. It is truly charming, with tin ceilings, hardwood floors, newly renovated bathroom & kitchen with stainless steel appliances. It also includes free wifi internet! Living room has a sofa bed and there is a daybed in the small room. Best suited for 2 or a family of 4. This is a 1.5 bed room apartment- 1 large bedroom with private den attached.

Located in the Bedstuy/ Clinton Hill neighborhood of Downtown Brooklyn. We're across the street from the C Train & S Shuttle that connects to the 2, 3, 4, 5, & Q trains. 2 blocks from the A Express train & the Long Island Railroad and near all major bus lines.

Close to shopping, theaters and tourist attractions including The Brooklyn Museum, Brooklyn Academy of Music, Brooklyn Botanical Garden, Brooklyn Public Library, Prospect Park and even just a subway ride to Coney Island. We are 1.4 miles from Pratt Art Institute.

Also in the neighborhood are tons of cafes & restaurants like these: Outpost Lounge,Kush Cafe, Joloff, Choice Market, Nice Pizza & Heavenly Crumbs Bakery.

15 minute train ride to Manhattan

$400 security & cleaning deposit is required at check-in, and will be refunded upon check-out provided the place is left the way it was found.

A $50 fee is charged for early check-in and late check out (when available).

Available for long-term rental.

Other things to note
Lincoln Market Grocery store is at the corner!

listing_name: Sunny, Modern room in East Village w City Views
description:
This clean and very comfortable private room in the East Village has spectacular views of downtown Manhattan and is a block away from Tompkins Square Park. Am always excited to share the space with guests! Thanks for taking a look.

The space
This listing is for 1 PRIVATE ROOM with a queen size bed in a shared luxury duplex with a large private terrace. Please note there is another guest room in the apartment.

The Apartment: Just one block off Tompkins Square Park in an elevator building. The apartment is a large luxury 2-floor, 3-bedroom, 2-bath duplex in Alphabet City/ East Village and a short walk away from Lower East Side, West Village, SoHo, Noho, Chinatown, Little Italy, and the rest of downtown Manhattan!

The Room: A large, bright room with a very comfortable full-sized bed, AC, large closet, and spectacular views of lower Manhattan.

The Hosts: Friendly, creative female who loves to travel, write, make movies, listen to music, and meet new people. My husband, who is a doctor, also lives in the apartment and loves to travel and chat about movies. I've lived in the neighborhood for over 18 years and have lots recommendations for favorite coffee shops, bars, restaurants, bookstores, museums, and anything else you can think of in the city.

Feel free to write if you have any questions!

Guest access
There is a large flatscreen TV, Netflix, Wi-Fi Internet, laundry in building, and a gym. There's also an absolutely enormous private terrace, with spectacular views of the city!

During your stay
Unless traveling for work, we'll usually meet at least when you arrive. But I'm always available to give recommendations and help out any way I can.

Other things to note
Please note this room shares a bathroom with the other guest room.

Also note that the terrace is upstairs outside the hosts bedroom, please be aware of noise and privacy.

Only people who have booked the room can use the terrace. Please no guests allowed.

Last, the apartment is about a 10/15 minute walk to the L train, although there is a bus a block away that goes directly to the L.
Thanks.

listing_name: Charming Room in Amazing East Village Apt. (B)
description:
The apartment is among the best and most unique available in the lively East Village. It’s a second-floor walk up with a large kitchen, spacious living room, large back deck with bbq, wi-fi, pressed-tin ceilings, roof access, etc. It has a lot of character; a very homey but spacious feeling with high ceilings, lots of artwork, plants, books, hardwood floors, exposed brick, etc.

The space
This is one of three rooms in a large 3-bedroom apartment inhabiting the second floor of a classic brick building in the East Village.

Room B is inviting and cozy with a desk for remote work. The bed is very comfortable and the room stays warm in the winter and cool in the summer.

The main inhabitants of the apartment are two male Americans, a photographer and a writer, so you will be sharing the common spaces with one or both of us. The apartment has a warm, authentic feel with a lot of history and we are looking for good people to share it with!

Guest access
Guests have access to the all of the common spaces, including the back deck, kitchen, living room, bathroom, etc.

During your stay
We are helpful, sociable and pleasant but also busy in our own lives. We just ask that guests are respectful of our time and space and we will do the same!

Other things to note
Just to be clear, you would be renting a room out in our 3 bedroom home. If you're looking for a slightly more anonymous hospitality experience, perhaps a hotel room would be a better fit! Staying with us will give you the experience of what it feels like to live with engaged locals in a classic apartment in one of the most interesting neighborhoods in the world. But if it is bespoke amenities and a concierge service that you require, this is not for you.

It should also be noted that this is not the quietest apartment, nor the quietest building nor the quietest neighborhood. It is in the center of the action so please expect the possibility of a certain amount of chaos. We all do are best to keep the apartment quiet from 10pm-9am but sh*t happens, so please understand that you will not be living in a vacuum.

listing_name: Private, sunny two-bedroom near subway & shops
description:
Enjoy peace and quiet and the comforts of home in a vibrant neighborhood with easy access to Manhattan!

You'll have this sunny, quiet, recently renovated third floor suite all to yourself, with kitchen, living room, two bedrooms, and private bathroom. Hosts live on site and are available to help.

We’re in a lively part of Astoria, with plenty of shops, bars, and restaurants to enjoy. After a short walk to the Ditmars N/W stop, the train is 15-20 minutes to Manhattan.

The space
This is a hosted rental with the host present; we live on the lower two floors and your space is on the third floor. Aside from sharing a front door and entryway, the space is completely private.

With two bedrooms, one with a queen and one with a double bed, the space is perfect for families or for friends traveling together. The larger bedroom can be opened to the living room to let in light, or closed off with doors and curtains for complete privacy. It has a wardrobe for storage and a desk for remote work; the second bedroom is cozy with a large closet.

The kitchen is well equipped for cooking including full-sized refrigerator, four-burner gas stove, and dishwasher, and it's stocked with dishes and essentials. Our FiOS internet is very fast for work or entertainment, and the Roku TV provides all the major streaming services (Netflix, Apple TV, Hulu, HBO Max, Disney+). A bluetooth speaker lets you enjoy your own music, and there's a mini-library of quirky NYC guidebooks, histories, and novels to explore.

We have three sons and we'll gladly provide you with any baby equipment that you need (portable crib, high chair, baby tub, toys and books, etc.).

Guest access
NYC prohibits listing full apartments as short-term rentals. Though this property is listed as a room, it is a full guest suite on the top floor of our townhouse. You will not be sharing the spaces described and pictured with anyone else. Our family lives on the lower floors of the house, but apart from sharing the front door and the entryway, the spaces are separate.

During your stay
We live downstairs and are generally available for any questions about the apartment or the neighborhood. We try to be available for your check-in, but a lockbox is available if that doesn't work out.

Other things to note
Access to the apartment is up two flights of stairs, so it's not a good place for anyone with mobility challenges.

We try to space bookings out by a day or more, so we can almost always accommodate early check-in and/or late check-out; just let us know your schedule!

NYC now restricts short-term stays to no more than two guests in a single property; if you need lodging for more, please get in touch. We may be able to arrange a space nearby for additional people.

The kitchen area is directly over our bedroom, so we do ask that you keep activity there to a minimum late at night. Noise from living room and bedrooms is not an issue.

We do not access phones/internet from sundown Friday to sundown Saturday except for emergencies; if you contact us via Airbnb during that time, we'll respond Saturday night or Sunday morning.

description:
Tastefully and elegantly furnished, spacious sun filled private NYC 1 BR Condo on a quiet tree lined street in a safe neighborhood. Perfect for your visit to New York City.

Experience New York City like a local.

Located within a five minute walk (three blocks) from the nearest subway (# 2 & # 3 trains) ... 15-20 minutes fromTimes Square/Penn Station/Grand Central/Port Authority.

A ten minute walk to other subways (A/B/C/D and 4/5/6) and also the Metro North to Westchester and Connecticut.

The space
A large and sunny private 1 bedroom condo in Manhattan on a quiet tree lined street in a safe neighborhood.

Ideal for travelers & tourists who want to experience living in New York. 15-20 minutes from Penn Station/Grand Central/Times Square/Port Authority Bus Terminal. 5-10 minute walk to the 2/3/4/5/6/A/B/C/D subway trains.

The apartment is in a quiet and safe neighborhood and is a very short distance from Museum Mile (the Guggenheim, The Metropolitan, Cooper-Hewitt), Central Park and Columbia University

Harlem is a multi-cultured neighborhood that is home to the Schomburg Center For Research In Black Culture, The Apollo Theater, The Studio Museum of Harlem, The Cotton Club, Aaron Davis Hall, Red Rooster, Chez Lucienne, Corner Social, Sylvia's, The Dance Theater Of Harlem, & The Maysles Institute Film Center, The Cloisters & The Hip Hop Cultural Center.

Area amenities supermarket/grocery, pharmacy, banks/atm, restaurants, wine shop, bars & pubs etc. are within a few blocks of the apartment.

The apartment includes internet access, flat screen tv with cable, hi-fi with ipod dock, clean sheets, towels, blow drier, iron and board and coffee maker.

Guest access
Guest has access to all areas

description:
Close to Times Square!!! Bars, bakeries, cafés, night life, upscale boutiques, restaurants, The Apollo Theatre, Museum Mile, Yankee Stadium, Central Park, and much more!
Quiet, well equipped, well maintained, spacious apartment in historic, landmark property, in Historic Sugar Hill.
Neighborhood is well appointed, within close proximity to the center and saturated with amenities. Serviced by three metro stations, total of six train lines; the main hub is less than a two minute walk away.

The space
Private Room in spacious tranquil apartment.
Historic property is situated on a bluff, overlooking a long stretch of gorgeous park, on a quaint, tranquil and intimate street.

700 capacity swimming pool right in front of the apartment and newly renovated amphitheater--Free outdoor jazz concerts, featuring world renowned artists, part of the city's Summer Stage and Summer in the City Series,June-September.

Renovated apartment in landmark property.
Spacious 1,000 square feet. Very clean, well maintained, well equipped, super quiet and peaceful.

*AMENITIES*

-Elevator building.

-QUEEN bed, sleeps two persons. Television with premium cable plus HBO and Showtime. Desk, lamp, air conditioner during summer, and spacious closet.

-One-and-a-half Baths, the room has a private half bath.

-Upon arrival: Complimentary bottled water in your room.

-Coffee, Assorted Teas & Hot Cocoa available around the clock, self-serve.

-TOILETRIES: toothbrush, body wash, toothpaste, shampoo, conditioner, body lotion, hand sanitizer.

Professionsal ironing board and two irons in your room.

-Two blow dryers in the room, one in each bathroom.

-QUEEN Bed. Four hundred thread count sheets.
Orthopedic Martha Stewart pillows are changed 4x yearly.
All bedding is soaked in hospital grade sanitizer.
Room is thoroughly professionally steam cleaned and sanitized after each and during guest stays; I own an industrial steam cleaner.

-Full use of well equipped kitchen, with some basic food staples included.
(Cooking oil; condiments; rice)

-FREE High Speed Wireless Internet Access

-CONCIERGE: help with sight-seeing, directions, tourist books, maps and cur

-Winter months: the apartment is well heated throughout. A ceramic heater is available in the room, if additional heating is needed.

-Summer months: Apartment is air conditioned 24/7. Air conditioner in bedroom.

##############################

Conveniently located very close by:

-Starbucks

-Large Supermarket two blocks east of the apartment

-Organic Farmer's market, from July thru November down the street

-Duane Reade

-Two 24 hr Dunkin Donuts

-Several Banks: Chase Bank, Bank of America, Citi Bank, Carver Federal and Capitol Bank.

-Post Office

-Mail box two doors up

-Public Library

-A variety of several restaurants and bars within walking distance. Restaurant Row is close by.

-Gorgeous Riverside Drive..
River Bank State Park and promenade, right on the Hudson, within walking distance.

-Within walking distance of Columbia University

-Catch and release fishing at the Harlem Meer
-Waterfront and promenade on the Hudson River also known as the North River at Riverbank State Park

-You would be located just steps away from three (3) different metro stations. Just a few minutes from midtown. 59th Street/Columbus Circle/Central Park South, are only two express train stops away! Several buses and taxi service are also conveniently located very close by.

-Upon Booking: Whether you will be arriving by plane, train or bus, you will receive directions to the apartment.

-You will also receive suggested places to see, things to do, dining and, shopping.

-For those who drive, there is a parking garage two doors down. Rate: $8.45 plus tax, from 7:00am to 7:00pm, along with several other reasonably priced parking garages very close by.
FREE Street Parking is easy to procure.

-Flexible with check-in and check-out times.
As a COURTESY: If departing on a late flight, you can leave your luggage at the apartment, while you get the last of your sight-seeing/shopping accomplished.
This amenity must be arranged in advance.

-Multi-function Center: Printer, Copier, and Scanner, available for your use.


Come explore Sugar Hill's architectural beauty!
Apartment located in gorgeous, historic Sugar Hill.

Cathedral Church of St. John the Divine built in 1888...largest gothic cathedral in the world.
Columbia University
Apollo Theater

Bryant Park...FREE outdoor movies and arts & culture all summer long.

Easy access to Jersey Gardens Outlet Mall.

I look forward to hosting you!

description:
Come stay in the best location in Williamsburg! You will be just a few blocks away from restaurants, movie theaters, bars, gift shops, book stores as well as high end chain stores. There are plenty of Gyms, Pilates and Yoga studios. Domino Park on the waterfront that has spectacular views of Manhattan is two blocks away.

Enjoy all the comforts of home while you explore one of the hippest neighborhoods in Brooklyn, if not in the world.

The space
The small apartment is on the lower level of our town house and is completely private with it's own entrance. There is a queen sized bed in the bedroom and and an American Leather full sleeper sofa in the living room.

There is a small kitchenette that has what you need to prepare meals with pots, pans, dishes, silverware, toaster, stove, oven, and a refrigerator. For your coffee needs we have a coffee maker, percolator and French press.

The bathroom is large with a walk in shower. We supply you with towels and linens. For our guests that have an extended visit with us we are happy to wash your linens and towels weekly. A laundry mat is a few blocks away so visitors can take care of their personal laundry. There is also an iron with a mini ironing board and we can loan you our vacuum cleaner .

This is urban living with low ceilings so you will hear foot steps, furniture moving about and the occasional bark from our pup above.

Guest access
Everything you need from shopping to entertainment to transportation are all in walking distance to the apartment. There is street parking but cars have to be moved daily for street cleaning.

Other things to note
There are two sets of steps to access the apartment. One set of two the other set of three both have handrails.

description:
The space
If it's an authentic New York experience you're looking for, you've found it. Located in the heart of the East Village of Manhattan, this one bedroom apartment is as cool as the city and as cozy as the country. Perfect for couples or singles. Not suitable for families. East & west facing windows wash the flat in lots of morning and afternoon light, flat screen tv, internet, queen size tempurpedic bed (with memory foam mattress!), laundry/dry cleaners on ground floor, kitchen, full bath.
I collect early American antiques which impart a country charm to this small yet beautiful city apartment.

10-15 minute walk to the F train; 15-20 minutes to N/R & 6 lines. Beautiful historic Tompkins Square Park is just blocks away and anchors the colorful diversity of the neighborhood. The East Village abounds with a bar and cafe culture unmatched in other parts of Manhattan. If you were to throw a stone from my roof the arch of it's trajectory would pass no less than several upscale restaurants and downscale dive bars. The only neighborhood in New York!

Everything you will need is provided. Towels, linens, toiletries, umbrellas if it rains, books (but why read when there's so much to do?) etc. All you have to do is show up.

Note:
Apartment is on the 5th floor of an old tenement building. No elevator. The stairs are no joke. If you bring lots of luggage you're in for a challenge. You wanted an authentic New York experience right? You'll have the best legs south of 14th St. when you leave!

The building is heated in winter by steam heat. The pipes can rattle when hot water is flowing thru them. Common in Lower East Side tenements.

Weekends can be noisy. That's what happens when you're surrounded by amazing bars and fantastic restaurants.

Guest access
Entire apartment

The space

description:
A modern apartment in a quiet but fun area.

The space
This is a large 1-bedroom with balcony. Both the apartment and furnishings are
brand new, including:

A modern bathroom with heated floor.

Central air conditioning, intercom, tv, and phone. Internet (wireless and wired) and utilities are included.

A full washer/dryer, and an eat-in kitchen with dishwasher.

A utility room with storage.

A bright bedroom with a comfortable queen bed and plenty of closet space.

A balcony looking north toward Manhattan.

A living area with comfortable furniture and additional storage, including a futon folding out to an additional full bed.

steps to the intersection of 3 subway lines,
20 minutes to downtown Manhattan, 10 minutes to beautiful Prospect Park. The apartment is extremely quiet and bright -- a great place to work or rest!

listing_name: Bright Room With A Great River View
description:
Come and enjoy a cozy, sunny room with a great view of the Hudson River and the George Washington Bridge. Room includes semi private entrance and a half bath in suite. Just one block from the subway

The space
Come and enjoy a bright sunny cozy room in a 3 bedroom classic prewar building on Riverside Drive in upper Manhattan. The room has a great view of the Hudson River and the George Washington Bridge.

This room is ideal for students or traveling adults looking for a comfortable temporary and affordable accommodation.

The room is petite and is best suited for 1 person It has a semi private entrance with its own 1/2 bathroom. It's a large apartment by New York standards with 2 entrances. One entrance leads almost directly into the bedroom. This is great for the guest as your comings and goings don't bother anyone at all. The 1/2 bath has its own sink and toilet allowing for your additional privacy.

I will provide bedding, linens and towels as needed.
There is a laundry facility available for use 7 days per week in the basement of the building from 8am to 9pm.

The apartment is located in front of Riverbank State Park offering a swimming pool, track, gym, soccer field, basketball courts, tennis courts, skating and an amazing bike path that stretches the length of Manhattan.

There are several good restaurants in the neighborhood, a drugstore on the corner, a supermarket and several banking machines apx. a block away.

description:
Classic brownstone apartment in historic Brooklyn. Independent unit with separate entrance.
Charming large master bedroom + smaller separate den room. Both rooms have separate access to the bathroom. Fully equipped kitchen. Living/dining room.

The space
This charming and spacious 1+ bedroom apartment is located on a quiet block in the heart of the historical section of Stuyvesant Heights, Brooklyn. The apartment is on the second floor of an owner occupied brownstone and it was recently renovated and fully furnished. The owner is an Italian woman from Florence, Italy with 20 years experience of New York (yes, we speak Italian!). Roberta is on hand to give advice about the neighborhood, make recommendations and give directions.

The large master bedroom has a queen sized bed (UK king,) while the smaller through room has a sofa bed that is also a queen size.

Lots of the original details such as stained glass skylights, fire place mantels and moldings were saved during the renovations. The living room and kitchen windows face the building's backyard that was also upgraded with a patio and lots of planters and flowers. The visitors will have access to the unit from an independent entrance, completely separate from the owner's apartment.

The apartment is located only 5 blocks from the subway station and only 5 stops from downtown Manhattan on the express A train, that also guarantees easy transfers to and from JFK airport. The area offers Zagat rated restaurants (Saraghina/Italian, Peaches/Southern-American, Besos/Latin) and other entertainment facilities such as Therapy Wine Bar, Casablanca Bar, Manny's Coffee Shop, Manny's Bar and Saraghina Tapas Bar and Saraghina's Bakery, among others. There is a local supermarket plus convenience stores on almost every corner, some of which offer organic products. We are very close to the shopping strip on Fulton Street that offers more markets, banks and a large variety of products, restaurants and bars. The historical tree-lined Stuyvesant Avenue (1 block,) McDonough Avenue (2 blocks) and Decatur Avenue offer some of the best examples of Brooklyn architecture from the late 1800's (brownstones and mini mansions.) The Brooklyn Botanical Garden, the Brooklyn Museum and the wonderful Prospect Park are also very easy to reach via subway.

Guest access
Full apartment

Other things to note
The bathroom offers a shower only!
The small bathtub in the bathroom is not suitable for taking baths. When the tub is filled with water, the overflow mechanism produces a substantial leak into the apartment below mine, which is causing extensive damage to ceiling and walls.

The use of candles, incense, aromatic oils and any other product/device that needs combustion to work is strictly forbidden as their use represent a safety hazard.

listing_name: Historical space with exceptional light & art
description:
Share our beautiful home in landmarked Park Slope. Enjoy your Brooklyn stay with us as hosts in our unit. The home is a historical building with rare windows on 3 sides with natural light & a terrace overlooking interior gardens. All amenities outside your doorstep: restaurants, shops, playgrounds, & subways.

The space
Parlor Floor
Enter the unit at the top of the exterior stairs. This is called the 'Parlor Floor' and it has 13 feet high ceiling and original 19th century details. We have added 21st century comfort, convenience, and design with quality contemporary furnishings while preserving the historical details throughout the home.

In the middle parlor, you can hang out in the art filled room with original white marble fireplaces or pull down the film screen and project a movie. In the front parlor, you are welcome to play piano or sit and enjoy on the sun drenched sofa and armchairs. There is a half bath on this floor.

As you go up and down the stairs, you will travel on the elegant mahogany staircase to the 2nd floor.

This floor is fully equipped for cooking and eating in the 500 sq ft dining room with 4 large windows. Note, we do NOT have a dishwasher.

You can enjoy the 200 sq ft outdoor terrace overlooking the interior gardens from this floor, dine outside or perhaps have a bbq. The terrace is accessible either through the window in the bathroom or the right most window in the back office.

The 300sq foot office space is off the kitchen and you are welcome to use as you need.

There is an alcove off the dining room with a Smart TV. There is a full bathroom with a window on this floor.

In the summer months on this floor, we have 2 window AC units installed for comfort. Please do turn off the ACs when not in use or when you leave.

On the top floor of house are 2 bedrooms for your use with full private bathroom and a window overlooking the terrace for your use while you stay with us.

The master blue bedroom has a spacious bed with a king size mattress and faces the back garden. The white bedroom (double size bed) has views to Manhattan and the fabulous NYC sunsets.

Fresh linens and towels are provided. In the summer months, each of the bedrooms has an AC unit.

We hope you will enjoy the art through the home made by my husband. Our home is child (not baby) friendly with some games available.

Wifi is available throughout, and Washer/Dryer available for use which is in the cellar.

There is absolutely no smoking inside the house, no pets are allowed, and please take off your shoes while in the house.

Please let me know if you have any questions about the home, Brooklyn, or specific needs and interests during your stay. I love to help new families and groups discover the best of Brooklyn and NYC and get the most from their stays.

Guest access
Enjoy comfort and the perfect location in a Brooklyn traditional brownstone.

During your stay
I am always reachable for questions, but prefer to leave you to have your own adventures. My adult daughter will be in and out of the house during your stay.

Other things to note
Near subway lines (Q/B, 2/3, R). SoHo in 15 min, Times Sq in 30 min. Walk to Barclays Center or Prospect Park, the most luxurious nature in Brooklyn.

listing_name: Cozy authentic residence near Central Park
description:
We are back - renovated and better!
Only for long-term (30+ days) reservations, check out the discounts.
Old world meets new in this stunning cozy two-leveled mini-loft condo. The apartment is a part of landmarked NYC brownstone located in the heart of historic Harlem. Meticulously restored original brick walls, fireplace mantel, stained glass coincide with artistic custom staircase, stainless steel, brand new kitchen appliances and all other modern amenities in this amazing home.

The space
Old world meets new in this stunning cozy two-leveled mini-loft condo with private entrance from the front yeard on the ground level. The apartment was fully renovated in 2019 and is a part of landmarked and famous type of New York turn-of-the-20th-century brownstone located in the heart of historic Harlem. Meticulously restored original brick walls, fireplace mantel, stained glass coincide with artistic custom staircase connecting two levels of the apartment, stainless steel kitchen appliances and all modern amenities in this amazing home.

The apartment is located in a splendidly cool neighborhood. You will immediately feel that unique old New York vibe in an area that is safe, and getting more and more internationally diverse. It is simply loved by the Europeans! Selecting this apartment will allow you to feel New York with all its history. A great convenient location will let you enjoying a very unique and quiet neighborhood feel in the middle of New York city, while being 15 minutes train ride from the Midtown and just a half-block away from the bustling city life.

Surrounding Area.

This is a special opportunity to live on a beautiful landmarked residential block steps to 2/3 train, 2 parks (Central Park and Marcus Garvey Park), the celebrity chiefs’ Red Rooster restaurant, WholeFoods market and multiple weekend farmer markets. Much loved by the locals Settepani, Barawine restaurants and Caffe Latte coffee shop are located on the corner of the block.

The whole area around Marcus Garvey Park captures the authentic energy that is exclusive to Harlem. Featuring free open-air jazz concerts in the summer, it truly represents the spirit of this part of the city. There are many places featuring all kinds of music and art in the area. Not to mention that the house itself literally is being adjacent to the former house of Hammerstein, the famous Broadway composer.

Interior features.

-2 levels -loft and recreational area - connected by staircase
- Lower level could be used as an office
- Upper level has open yet fully separate queen-size bed area
-1.5 baths
-Wahser/dryer on premises
-Dining area
-Exposed brick
-Hardwood floors
-Fireplace
-Custom lighting
-Storage space
-Top-of-the-line kitchen and baths
-Central air conditioning and heat, zoned to individual temperature
-Complete set of linens, towels, kitchen and bath equipment

We do not rent to more than 2 people.
Available only for long stays over 30 days.
Cleaning available on demand.
Utilities included.

Amazing 2 bedroom, 2 bathroom loft on a quiet street in the heart of the Lower East Side. The apartment is colorful, comfortably designed, and very homey.

A comfortable, colorful 1000 sq ft., second story walk up on the lower east side of New York City! Master bedroom (queen), second bedroom (single+trundle), and living room. listing_name: Cozy private room and bathroom in a Brownstone
description:
You will feel at ease and a part of the Harlem neighborhood while residing in a large, beautiful and quiet private guest room in a brownstone, equipped with washer and dryer. The only shared space is the entrance and common hallway.

The space
West 119th Guest Suite

The 600 sq ft guest suite is well lit and furnished with 1 queen size bed and futon. The space is furnished with: cable TV, wireless internet, A/C, original hardwood floors, a decorative fireplace, 12 ft ceilings, ceiling fan and 96% of the original details from 1910. The well lit bathroom is very spacious with the original claw foot tub for showering or soaking. There is an alcove area across from the tub with a leather bench for pampering after bathing. There is a laundry closet with a stackable washer & dryer and fully stocked linen closet in the bathroom.


Please note, you are responsible for all food.

You are 3 blocks from the 2 & 3 trains, which are express trains making the following stops downtown: W.110th St., 96th St., 72nd St., 42nd St., 34th St. and 14th St. There are 2 food markets and plenty of great restaurants, all within walking distance.

Finally, I guarantee you will be please with your stay.

Guest access
The beautiful front doors of the building and 1 flight of stairs in the common area.

During your stay
I own the building and live in the apartment below the guest apartment.
I am very accommodating and fair.
My motto - I am here to make you happy.

Other things to note
I am very strict about safety and security of the building. The 2 entry doors must be locked at all times. Guests are given 2 sets of keys. 2 sets are expected at checkout. If a set is loss, $1000 will be required to replace both locks on entrance doors and 8
replacement keys. Keys must be handed directly to me at checkout. Please do not leave in room.

description:
Doug has been hosting since 2010 and lives in Brooklyn’s vibrant cultural hub. Guests stay in a private room within a shared owner-occupied apartment with Doug, near top restaurants, cafes, and theaters. The Apple Store and all major subway lines, including the LIRR, are close to Atlantic/Barclays and Ft. Greene Park. It's only 20 minutes to Manhattan. The room accommodates two, and the second guest receives a discount.

The space
Please read all of the descriptions about my home, reserve a room, or contact me with any questions you might have. The fee for the first guest is posted on the ABB site and is half-price for the second guest, which is also listed. Sorry, no parties, film shoots, smoking, toking, or vaping in the bedrooms or the backyard. Thanks for checking out my listing!

I live in the hip and historic brownstone neighborhood of Fort Greene, Brooklyn. It's a quiet and relaxing location, only fifteen minutes by car or twenty minutes by subway to SOHO, Manhattan, or other popular areas of New York City. The LIRR and Flatbush/Atlantic Terminal are within a few minutes' walking distance from the house. Both national and international airports are conveniently accessible by car, taxi, subway, or railroad. Generally, it is easy to find parking on the street in front of or near the building.

Shopping markets, an Apple Store, a 365 Whole Foods, the Barclays Center Arena, the Brooklyn Academy of Music (BAM), many exceptional cafes and restaurants, and Fort Greene Park are easily accessible on foot. Guests share the apartment with Doug and have their own private room. They can use the backyard (but not for parties), the wireless computer connection, and the full bathroom. The full and half bathrooms are shared with Doug. Cooking facilities for guests may be used on a limited basis. Guests can not bring other people to stay in the apartment overnight. Drinking non-alcoholic beverages is fine, but inviting friends/family to socialize is not permitted. Each room has a passage lock but the doors are not keyed according to NY state housing law. There's usually free street parking but you may have to wait or drive around for a legal spot. I would love to socialize with all my guests all the time; however, I wouldn’t get my own work done. Please note that my office is upstairs and the space isn’t available for lounging. I appreciate your understanding.

Guest access
The entrance to apartment #1 is on the garden street level underneath the stoop by the brown buzzer. Guests share the apartment with Doug and have their own private bedroom with access to the downstairs full bathroom, wi-fi, half bathroom on the first floor and the backyard. For guests staying longer than one week, the kitchen is available on a limited basis after discussing their needs with me. Guests are welcome to use the fridge.

During your stay
Doug greets guests when they arrive, assists them with directions and makes suggestions for various events, art venues and places to visit. His specialty is the visual arts and museums.

Other things to note
The brown buzzer is underneath the stoop/garden level and guests do not have to go up the steps to ring the stainless steel buzzer. Guests are not allowed to have overnight company or friends/family socialize at the house and no parties or pets. For elderly guests or those who require special accommodations for accessibility, unfortunately, my home is not appropriate as there are steps to access my apartment and numerous steps to access the first-floor bathroom and kitchen.

description:
Doug has been hosting since 2010 and lives in the cultural center of Brooklyn. Guests share the apartment with Doug and have their own private room. There are excellent restaurants and cafes, BAM, an Apple Store, Whole Foods, and more nearby. All subways and the LIRR/Atlantic Mall-Barclay Arena are a 5-minute walk and only 20 minutes to Manhattan. Ft Greene Pk is down the block, and Metro Tech is nearby. Two guests can stay in the same room, and there's a discounted charge for the second guest.

The space
Please read all of the descriptions about my home, reserve a room, or contact me with any questions you might have. The fee for the first guest is posted on the ABB site and is half-price for the second guest, which is also listed. Sorry, no parties, film shoots, smoking, or vaping in the bedrooms or the backyard. Thanks for checking out my listing!

I live in the hip and historic brownstone neighborhood of Fort Greene, Brooklyn. It's a quiet and relaxing location only fifteen minutes by car or twenty minutes by subway to SOHO, Manhattan, or other popular areas of New York City. The LIRR and Flatbush/Atlantic Terminal are within a few minute's walking distance from the house. Both national and international airports are convenient by car, taxi, subway, or railroad. Generally, there is easy to find parking on the street in front of or near the building.

Shopping markets, an Apple Store, a 365 Whole Foods, the Barclay's Center Arena, Brooklyn Academy of Music (BAM), many exceptional cafes and restaurants, and Fort Greene Park are easily accessible on foot. Guests share the apartment with Doug and have their own private room. They can use the backyard (but not for parties), the wireless computer connection, and the full bathroom. The full and half bathrooms are shared with Doug. Cooking facilities for guests may be used on a limited basis. Guests can not bring other people to stay in the apartment overnight. Drinking non-alcoholic beverages is fine but inviting friends/family to socialize is not permitted. Each room has a passage lock but the doors are not keyed according to NY state housing law. There's usually free street parking but you may have to wait or drive around for a legal spot. I would love to socialize with all my guests all the time however, I wouldn’t get my own work done. Please note that my office is upstairs and the space isn’t available for lounging. I appreciate your understanding.

Guest access
The entrance to apartment #1 is on the garden street level underneath the stoop by the brown buzzer. Guests share the apartment with Doug and have their own private bedroom with access to the downstairs full bathroom, wi-fi, half bathroom on the first floor and the backyard. For guests staying longer than one week, the kitchen is available on a limited basis after discussing their needs with me. Guests are welcome to use the fridge.

During your stay
Doug greets guests when they arrive, assists them with directions and makes suggestions for various events, art venues, and places to visit. His specialty is the visual arts and museums.

Other things to note
The brown buzzer is underneath the stoop/garden level and guests do not have to go up the steps to ring the stainless steel buzzer. Guests are not allowed to have overnight company or friends/family socialize at the house and no parties or pets. For elderly guests or those who require special needs for accessibility, unfortunately, my home is not appropriate as there are steps to access my apartment and numerous steps to access the first-floor bathroom and kitchen.

description:
Stay in the Heart of NYC. A 15 minute walk to Times Square, you'll be staying in one of the hippest neighborhoods in Manhattan -- Hell's Kitchen!!

** I'm also Fully Vaccinated. I had my Pfizer double-shot. My 2nd dose was April 1st, 2021. I've also been triple-boosted**

The space
Your Fully Private Room has one comfortable queen size loft bed 🛌 with a Tuft & Needle mattress. It also includes:

* 46” Samsung HD Smart 3D LED TV 📺 (with Netflix, Amazon Prime Video & YouTube Premium)
* Samsung 3D Smart Blu-ray Disc Player (with 3D glasses!)
* WiFi (200 Mbps)
* Mini-Fridge
* Air Conditioning / Heat
* Freshly cleaned sheets, pillows, blankets, bath towels & washcloths
* NYC Guidebooks, Maps and a Native New Yorker's knowledge of cool local 'off-the-beaten-path' stuff to do
* Rotating DISCO 🪩 💃🏽 🕺🏿 BALL & Light!
* 2 Arcade1Up PartyCades with 18 games!!

Most of my visitors are couples, family or good friends who don't mind sharing the bed together. However, I do have a comfortable Intex twin-size air mattress available (BY ADVANCE REQUEST ONLY), but it does make the space in the room a bit tight.

The room is clean, quiet and dark at night. I say this because living in NYC that's often difficult to find with all the street noise, noisy neighbors and bright lights blaring in one's windows. Not the case at my place. You'll sleep 💤 very well.

I have two retro Arcade1Up PartyCades. They’re available for guests to play anytime for free. Some games can even be played together in two-player mode with my additional game controller 🎮 like Joust, Rampage and Street Fighter! 👊

Games included are Defender, Gauntlet, Klax, Rootbeer Tapper, Bubbles, Joust, Rampage, Wizard of War, Sinistar, Gorf, Street Fighter II: The World Warrior, Super Street Fighter II Turbo, Street Fighter II: Champion Edition, Super Puzzle Fighter II: Turbo, Strider, Captain Commando, Eco Fighters and Ghosts ‘n Goblins.

Although I have a fully stocked kitchen I prefer that my guests don’t use it to cook. You’re on vacation after all, visiting NYC, which is a gastronomical and cultural smörgåsbord. You have a mini-fridge in your room.

For a small fee I rent my bicycles 🚴(all 6 of them). Cheaper than renting at any bike shop, you can ride them all day/night too. I also lead easy, safe and fun Night Bicycling Tours of Central Park with Nightrider NYC, as well as other short tours by bike, foot and/or subway. I bicycle toured/camped across the USA twice and rode across Europe in '87, before cell phones and the internet made us all lazy. Let me know what your interests are and maybe I can put something together for you.

I LOVE ❤️ hosting & meeting new people & if I have the time I'll show you around my neighborhood - just ask. I've had some really fun experiences with my former guests.

You'll have some closet space w/hangars & a drawer in my dresser for your clothing, as well as a empty drawer in the WC for your toiletries 🧻 . I have many shampoos, conditioners & other assorted toiletries which you are welcome to use as well as laundry detergent (there's a Laundromat right next to my building).

I'm on the top 5th floor of a 5-story brownstone walk-up **THERE IS NO ELEVATOR**

I provide clean sheets, blankets, pillows & towels and have other assorted toiletries that you can use, including an iron & hairdryer. My place is clean - no bedbugs here!

**I'M A NON-SMOKER 🚭 & DON'T ALLOW SMOKING IN MY APARTMENT OR ANYWHERE ELSE IN THE BUILDING**

My check-in time is flexible, starting at noon, going until whenever you need to arrive. On a case-by-case basis I might be able to allow an earlier check-in time. Check-out is noon, but, again, on a case-by-case basis I might be able to allow a later check-out time.

I'm a big BROADWAY 🎭 buff. I see nearly every show that comes to NY. This could be a Very Expensive 💴 Hobby, but as I'm a New Yorker who comes from a theater background I have connections and can 'sometimes' get tickets MUCH CHEAPER than anywhere else. If you wanna see a show during your visit (and I wholeheartedly recommend you do so), let me know and I'll try and work my magic for you!

I’m also a member of these 3 following ticket sites that I pay a yearly membership fee to in order to get cheap tickets from: Play-by-Play, Clubfreetime and TDF . During your stay I’ll logon to show you the current offerings and get you ticks to something if you want. Also check out the sites BroadwayForBrokePeople and BwayRush to find out about the cheap Rush and Lottery policies for each Broadway 🎭 show.

If you're a museum junkie, visiting all the cultural institutions that NYC has to offer can be rather expensive 💰. Many places have a ‘suggested donation’ admission, but it’s only for New Yorkers. Don’t worry — I’ve got your back. I’ll give you my extra ID and you can use it to get the special deal. When I visit the MET or the Museum of Natural History (among others) I just pay $1 to get in. Check out this for more info: NYCforfree .

As for me, I'll be sleeping in the living room on the Murphy Wall Bed (see my photos for more into)

Got stinky undies? Well, wash 'em then! I have detergent & fabric softener you can use. A cheap & reliable Coin-Operated Laundromat is right next to my building. You can do it yourself or just drop it off to have it washed (usually done the same day/pay by the pound - or kilo for you krazy Europeans.)

Thanks for checking my place out. If you have any questions let me know!

** I'm also Fully Vaccinated 😷 . I had my Pfizer double-shot. My 2nd dose was April 1st, 2021. I've also been triple-boosted**

*** IF AVAILABLE, for an extra $50 per night for a booking of 2 people I can let you have my Full Private Apartment. If the booking is for 1 person it's an additional $65 per night***

Guest access
NOTHING is off-limits at my flat -- My place is Your Place! You can use my kitchen, just not at 3am! (you know, during Reasonable hours). You're welcome to use and relax in my front room, unless I'm there working or sleeping.

During your stay
During your stay you can be as private as you want, or come and socialize a bit with me. I like to show my guests around my neighborhood and, if they like, go out for a drink or two. I'm also an avid cyclist, and could possibly take you on a tour of Central Park (I have 6 bikes in the basement we can use). I'll be sleeping in the front room most nights and in-and-out during the day. If you want some ideas & suggestions on off-the-beaten-track stuff that us NYC locals do, just ask!!

Other things to note
Although Hell's Kitchen has a pretty sordid history, it's now one of the hippest neighborhoods in Madhattan! Full of great bars and diverse restaurants that stay open late, just a 15 minute walk to the Theatre District and Times Square, it's a safe, centrally located area where you'll find a lot to do and see for the entire family.

A bit about me:

I'm a Handyman/Writer/Actor/Bicycle Tour Guide and Amateur USBC Bowler trying to survive in the Concrete Jungle we call the Big Apple. I've been surviving here since 1984, so I must be doing something right!

description:
Private 350 Sq foot room with a private bathroom in a privately owned single family townhouse in Chelsea. You'll be living with an on-site family of artists who live in the same upper unit, so this is not a fake hotel like many Airbnb's have become but a temporary roommate situation. If you are not comfortable sharing a home with people this will not work. But if you are looking for a real cultural experience in a beautiful home this will be a good fit. PS We have a very friendly cat!

The space
Large furnished private bedroom available in NYC! Located in a beautiful brownstone in Chelsea on one of the most sought after tree lined blocks in Manhattan. You will be sharing the upper floors of the house with me and my family.

DISCLAIMER: If you are looking for a traditional B&B or a cheaper version of a hotel room, this may not be right for you. It is an old building with old fixtures and furniture, some of it a bit dog-eared. This is our longtime family home and we love hosting guests in our extra bedroom. The townhouse also has a studio for classes screenings and readings, and a film production office.

This historic artistic residence has hosted such luminaries as Tennessee Williams, Mya Angelou, Miles Davis and Allen Ginsberg. Steps away from all galleries, the Chelsea Piers and spectacular boutique restaurants. Blocks away from the Soho House, Chelsea Market and the chic Meat Packing District. Steps to the High Line! 1 1/2 blocks from the C/E Subway line.

Hardwood floors, working marble fireplace. Private bathroom with shower. High speed Internet, Fresh cotton towels, Weekly maid service and Utilities all included.

Presence is an arts institute featuring classes in acting for stage and film and presents readings by established and emerging playwrights and poets. Support the arts while spending a wonderful New Years Vacation in NYC!

Guest access
You will be sharing the upper multi-floor unit with myself and our family. Your bedroom has a working fireplace and a mini fridge for your use. There is a beautiful tiled bathroom with shower that is just for you. Our kitchen is reserved for our family use but feel free to make use our Nespresso machine coffee or make water for tea. We live in the same unit and so are on site and can respond to any questions. Our bedroom is one floor above yours (note the top three floors are one continuous apartment) and our private event space is one floor below. We often have cultural events on site which you are welcome to attend.

During your stay
We live in the same unit and we will be able to help immediately with any questions when not at work. If we are out we will be able to respond to questions rapidly though ther Airbnb app.

Other things to note
Note that this building, unlike other buildings in the neighborhood, has not been carved into small apartments. It is a two unit house, all occupied by family members. You will be sharing the upper three floors (unit 2) with myself and my family and our private event space, so be aware that there will be occasionally lots of activity in the home as we go about our lives as artists, poets and theater artists.

Overall, the combination of cleanliness, quietness, convenient amenities, and a well-connected location makes this space an attractive choice for guests looking for a comfortable and enjoyable stay.

Garden Oasis in Brooklyn’s Prospect Heights

This lovely Garden apartment is in our Townhouse. We own and live on the premises. Available for short-term at a daily rate. It’s fully furnished and an ideal place for visitors wanting the amenities of a home.

The private entire is just under the front stoop, making it an unassuming and modest seg-way to a tranquil retreat from the bustle of city life. The garden patio is perfect for reading, writing, and coffee.

This apartment offers complete privacy for your stay; the apartment is quiet and comfortable for sleeping two but designed for one a retreat from city life, while conveniently situated in the Prospect Heights section of Brooklyn, rated one of the top neighborhoods to live in New York City! We ask that you use the local postal annex for mail or packages, not our home address.

This is not a HOTEL, it’s my home. I have personal items in the apartment.

Jameelah's on Greene is a fully renovated SMOKE FREE, 2 bedroom apartment nestled in the heart of the Bedford Stuyvesant enclave of Brooklyn, NY with amenities galore!

An ideal option for business travelers, academics and anyone who doesn't want to pay $300+ a night for a tiny room! This is an apartment set in a 4-story walk-up, 4-family, century-old Brooklyn brownstone in a vibrant working-class neighborhood.

Your home away from home is located 3 short blocks from the mass transit J, Z , M train line which can then whisk you to all points in Manhattan within minutes. Street parking is always available, usually directly in front of the house.

This apartment is fully furnished with lots of natural sunlight and designed with you as the guest in mind. The apartment has cozy duvet covers and soft pillows, along with a Radio with iPod/iPad playing capabilities Jameelah's can accommodate up to 4 guests (6 if you don't mind sharing beds/air mattress)

A fully functional kitchen awaits you where you’ll find dishes and cookware for all your epicurean desires. The apartment is equipped with several coffee makers so you are always free to enjoy an espresso and French Press or whatever your heart desires! Now these are perks!


The Penthouse sleeps up to 4 people; there are two full size bed

AC during summer months keeps the place nice and cool.
Cabel TV-local channels
All Linens Provided
Cabinets Stocked with Essentials
Coffee Maker/Tea assortment

3 minute walk to J, Z train on Gates Av/Broadway or M train @ Myrtle
10 minute walk to A,C train (Utica station) A,C train takes you to Harlem, Midtown, Queens

Convenient to JFK, LaGuardia, & Newark Airports

Within close proximity to Canal Street(chinatown), Lower East Side, Village, Union Square, 34th St, 42nd St, Lower Manhattan - WTC Site, Century 21, Prospect Park, Fort Greene Park, Williamsburg, Bushwick, Greenpoint, Brooklyn Heights, & DUMBO. Great restaurants & cafes, markets, pharmacies, bustling night life, museums, & parks.


Come enjoy your home away from home,

Jameelah A.

Guest access
The home is available to you, enjoy!

Please note this IS NOT A HOTEL. This is my home so there are some of my personal items in the place. Please be respectful of my home as you would expect guest in your home.

listing_name: Great studio apt near Central Park
description:
Stay in peace and comfort in this modern, spacious studio apartment, situated just one block from central park, 4 blocks from the Natural History Museum, just across central Park to Museum Mile, The Metropolitan Museum of Art, Guggenheim etc.

The space
Stay in peace and comfort in this modern, spacious studio apartment, situated just one block from central park, 4 blocks from the Natural History Museum, just across central Park to Museum Mile, The Metropolitan Museum of Art, Guggenheim etc. and many many more, 1-2 blocks from several major subway and bus lines, and within walking distance to restaurants, shops and every convenience imaginable.

The apartment, located on the second floor of a walk-up building, has a fully equipped kitchen, bathroom with tub/shower and a sleeping loft with queen size bed. A double futon pulls out to create two distinct sleeping areas, and the grand piano makes this a perfect home-away-from-home for musicians and music lovers alike. listing_name: Lovely pied-à-terre, in an historic building
description:
Legally registered with the city of New York, This beautiful, completely private room with an ensuite bathroom and a private entrance is on the second floor of a 19th century building, in the historic district Bed Stuy.

There are many cafes, bars, and restaurants to explore, and a nearby subway station for easy transport to Manhattan.

Staying at this sweet pied-à-terre is the perfect way to explore the ever exciting New York City.

The space
The space

The totally private room with en-suit bathroom is located on the 2nd floor of a historical Brownstone, built circa 1890. You will have your own separate entrance for total privacy.

The room is tastefully and comfortably furnished, airy and sunlit, with high ceilings and a large window.

The large bed accommodates two and is ideal for a couple, or two good friends/close family.

The room has a split system Air-conditioner, wi-fi, and online channels include Netflix, Hulu, and HBO.
You have a mini refrigerator and an electric kettle with tea/coffee selection. The grocery store is right on the corner, and you can find very good restaurants, cafes and bars in the neighborhood. A Full service Laundromat is also around the corner.
I can guide you to the nice spots if you wish.

The Subway line C is about 7 minutes walk, and the A line is about 12 minutes walk away. Both will take you to lower Manhattan in 15 minutes, and to midtown in 30 minutes.

We are a quiet house, and you will be the only guest. I live on the premises, and available for any requests, questions and guidance.

Guest access
You will have total privacy. Only booked guests are allowed to stay in the room, unless previously arranged with me.

description:
Our apartment is a rare find in New York City - a very spacious cool loft, with three bedrooms - a big principal bedroom suite with bathroom available to you, and two other bedrooms on the other side of the apartment, with their own bathroom, which we use. We are in the awesome neighborhood of Greenwood Heights (aka South Park Slope), Brooklyn; 30 minutes from Manhattan and with plenty of local activities.

We are officially registered with the city of New York for short-term rentals.

The space
The apartment is a 3rd floor walk up. There is a lot of space to cook, eat, work, and hang out, and there is even a laundry room in the apartment! Our big open kitchen has top-of-the-line appliances.

Guest access
You will have access to the principal bedroom suite. You will also have full access to a laundry room with a washer & dryer and drying rack as well as a big open kitchen with top-of-the-line appliances. If you prefer to not share a bed with your fellow traveler, we can provide a mattress for you to place on the bedroom floor.

During your stay
Our respectful and friendly children, my spouse, and I will be staying in the other two bedrooms, but because the apartment is so spacious, and we have our own bathroom, you will have plenty of privacy. We're happy to guide you as you explore Brooklyn and NYC, or give you all the privacy you need -- as you prefer!

Other things to note
BEFORE booking or inquiring, and especially if you do not already have positive reviews, please make sure you complete the verifications (especially the Offline one), and share something about yourself and what brings you to New York City.

This is an ELEVATOR building, high-speed wi-fi, Smart TVs in both the living and bedrooms including Sling TV for live tv. It’s also equipped with chargers, umbrellas, wireless printer and much more to make this your home away from home OR remote work space - with separate designated office area.

The bedroom is equipped with a fabulous Bear mattress King Bed, super soft linens & extra pillows. The living and bedroom have soundproof windows and bedroom has blackout curtains.

The well-organized & cozy galley kitchen has all upgraded appliances and is fully equipped with Nespresso machine, coffee maker, dishwasher, microwave, large fridge & more. There is a dining table for 4 and is fully outfitted for cooking.

The beautiful windowed marble-tiled bathroom has a bath/shower combination with amazing water pressure. All toiletries (see list below for details) are provided, and of course lots of towels.

The apartment is located 5 blocks from Central Park and just a 5-minute walk to an express subway station (2 stops from Times Square), minutes from Columbus Circle, Columbia University, Lincoln Center, Natural History Museum and a couple subway stops to Greenwich Village. And just across Central Park (walkable) you'll arrive at the Metropolitan Museum of Art.

There are many great markets & restaurants within a 5-block radius plus all the conveniences that NYC has to offer, in an incredibly safe neighborhood.

description:
This block is friendly, diverse, safe and a close quick commute to Manhattan, and has hosted many groups. You only share space with your group you travel with only. The house has 5 Full Bathrooms ,
8 +Bedrooms and is A few feet away from The King of New York BIG Mural. The 8 rooms are Class B multiple dwelling for short term. All rooms for short stays for 30 days or less/ w kitchen access. It’s great for groups who want to see NYC .Rooms have AC and 2 Gigs of WiFi for groups or families.

The space
This safe, clean, 4-story brownstone is located on the edge of the historic Clinton Hill/Bedford-Stuyvesant areas of Brooklyn.

The house features 24+ beds in spacious rooms and is equipped with 2 Gigs of Verizon FIOS WiFi, independent air conditioners, and heating units in each room to maximize guest comfort.

We provide affordable, clean, and legal housing accommodations, conveniently located 25 minutes from Midtown Manhattan via the A, C, or G subway lines.

This Class B Multiple Dwelling is fully compliant with safety regulations, featuring a centrally monitored sprinkler system to FDNY, smoke alarms, fire escapes, fire blankets and fire extinguishers. . Safety and comfort are our priorities. No smoking is allowed inside the house. Guests can prepare meals on-site.

The home offers a serene atmosphere with painted exposed brick walls in delicate yellow, creating a comfortable, inviting setting. We provide sheets, towels, and linens for your use, and all beds are real beds—no additional charges for extra guests. Parties and events are strictly prohibited.

Per Airbnb rules, social gatherings are limited to 16 people maximum (for reservations made after August 20, 2020). Larger Groups who are coming to enjoy NYC are always welcome so please inquire. We specialize in groups and have the capacity to host a variety of considerations.

This quiet, residential block offers the perfect base for exploring NYC while enjoying a peaceful retreat.

Guest access
8 Bedrooms,1 Living Room , 5 Bathrooms, kitchen access

Other things to note
Gordy does not change or adjust prices, for high demand seasons or holidays. We value and appreciate all holidays both U.S. and International so we stick with one simple price and ask that you have fun, smile a lot and enjoy the space. The house is for groups, and has everything on site to prepare for group meals, and linens are available on site as well as laundry facilities. We have 2 Gigs of wifi with Verizon Fios- we can hold over 40 devices.

Sunny, cute & quiet 1 bedroom features rooftop views & garden exposure. Wake up to birds singing! Comfy Tuft & Needle queen bed sleeps two, a cozy respite from bustling city. 1 block from High Line, Whitney Museum, Hudson River Park and Meatpacking- perfect location!
Please note the building does not allow animals.

The space
Please msg me before booking, may be flexible on dates.
Sunny, cute & quiet 1 bedroom features rooftop views & garden exposure. Comfy queen bed sleeps two, a cozy respite from bustling city. 1 block from High Line, Whitney Museum and Meatpacking- perfect location!

About the apartment...
This sunny, cute and cozy one bedroom features rooftop views and garden exposure. Stylishly designed, modern and crisp, very clean and tidy. It's ideal for a couple or individual, as the Queen size bed sleeps two. The bed is a brand new Tuft & Needle mattress, and it is beyond comfortable. Clean sheets and luxury bath towels are provided. High speed WiFi internet included. Gas stovetop, microwave, toaster and hot water boiler for your use.

About the building...
Pre-War Townhouse-style walk-up building. The apartment is on the top floor but there are only 3 flights of carpeted stairs, and it's a very easy walk-up. You may not want to pack a giant piece of luggage unless you have a friend helping you. I myself have carried a 50-lb. bag down the stairs, but have help getting it back up after trips. There are only 7 neighbors, so the building is very quiet. There are two front doors to the building with a buzzer for entry. Although there is no doorman, it is very secure and safe.
Please note the building does not allow animals.


About your host...
I am very hospitable and want to make sure you enjoy your stay. I am easily accessible even if I am away, so no worries about having questions for me. I am very responsive. The most important thing for me is to make sure you are comfortable!

Guests will have the full apartment to themselves.

Key pickup: We can arrange prior to your arrival.
I like to make sure guests are comfortable, so plan to hear from me upon check-in and text or email during your stay. I am accessible, so I would rather have a guest tell me they need something during their stay than say they didn't have it after they leave!

About the neighborhood...
Horatio Street is one of the most picturesque blocks in the West Village, steps from the best restaurants, the Meatpacking District, SoHo, TriBeCa and easily accessible (3 blocks walk) to 14th St Subway station (A,C,E,L) and 14th St. Subway Station (1, 2, 3). There is usually plenty of street parking in the neighborhood and there are also many parking garages available nearby.
Nearby restaurants: One block from Pastis, STK, Bagatelle, Standard Grill, Dos Caminos, Soho House and more!
Fabulous shopping: Jeffrey, Apple Store, Sephora, Theory, DVF, Vince, Warby Parker, Paige Denim, Rag & Bone, Intermix, Tory Burch, Hermes and more!
One block from Gansevoort Street entrance to the High Line and Whitney Museum!
Overall, a very safe and clean neighborhood that has everything to offer. Daytime strolls and shopping, and great nightlife.

Horatio Street is one of the most picturesque blocks in the West Village, steps from the best restaurants, the Meatpacking District, SoHo, TriBeCa and easily accessible (3 blocks walk) to 14th St Subway station (A,C,E,L) and 14th St. Subway Station (1, 2, 3). There is usually plenty of street parking in the neighborhood and there are also many parking garages available nearby.
The easiest way to get to my apartment from the airport (Newark, JFK or LGA) is to take a cab. From Newark, expect to pay $75, JFK $55, LGA $35 (excludes tip). I can provide directions vis Subway or cab when working out arrival details, just ask me!

Guest access
Guests will have the full apartment to themselves.

listing_name: Clinton Hill Duplex near Pratt w/Huge Patio!
description:
Top floor of a new build condo in a beautiful historic neighborhood in Brooklyn. Private, huge patio with skyline views. Quirky decor, adorable toddler, relaxed and respectful host. Newly remodeled spa-like bathroom. (Edited June 2024: cats mentioned glowingly in all the reviews have sadly passed away)

The space
Awesome room in a duplex apartment in a new build, elevator condo!

About the room: large and sunny, on the top floor of a duplex, penthouse apartment. The room has sliding glass doors which take you out to a private balcony - facing Manhattan with great skyline views! The room has everything you need: a queen sized bed, walk in closet, desk, TV/cable, wifi, dresser, mini fridge, keurig coffee machine. The room is on the top floor, above the living room, so very private and quiet. On the 7th floor, facing the back of the building, so there are no issues with traffic, or other noise. Sheets/bedding included, if you want, as well as hair dryer, shampoo, towels, and other basic house stuff you might need!

About the apartment: it's a 3 bedroom, 2 bathroom apartment. One bedroom is a master and is mine, one is a guest room where I people occasionally stay and the 3rd one, which is upstairs, I rent. It is a new build condo, with 2 bathrooms, so everything is modern and clean. I've included a floor plan you so you can see they layout. There is a dishwasher and also a washer and dryer in the apartment. The condo has an elevator, so don't worry about having to walk up 6 flights of stairs. :) As shown in the photos, there is a spiral staircase inside the apartment. If you have large luggage there is a secondary staircase you can use and access the bedroom from the roof.

About the neighborhood: I live in Clinton Hill, Brooklyn. This area has really become very trendy in the past few years that I have lived here. The apartment is located across the street from Pratt Institute, which is an Art College. Myrtle Ave is hopping and is lined with restaurants (Thai, Chinese, Sushi, Indian, Southern, Jamaican, Diners, French, Italian, everything!), bars, lounges, coffee shops, boutiques, etc... Also within a few blocks are 3 supermarkets (one organic, one not), a dry cleaners and a post office, two hip coffee shops, a small trendy grocer, a great neighborhood bar, a gas station, a dive bar and also a cool pub….. The area is very safe and trendy! The closet subway is the G train, at Classon. From there you can transfer easily to the A/C line, or the L and it is a short ride into downtown Manhattan. There is also a bus stop, literally, a block and a half from the building and from there it is a short ride to Downtown Brooklyn, where you can get every multiple subway lines.

About me: I am an English language teacher at a local university and I speak Italian, French, Spanish and German. I am a single mother by choice to a daughter born in March 2023. I am a relaxed, respectful person, and look forward to meeting you.

Any questions you have, don't hesitate to ask! :)

Guest access
Guest bathroom (newly private)- use of kitchen - fully furnished - washer and drier in unit

During your stay
I live with you in the apartment - the kitchen is obviously shared, and to some extent the guest bathroom, but with 2 bathrooms it rarely causes a conflict. It's great when I will see you - but I won't be in your hair! :)

description:
Fully furnished, private bedroom in the East Village ideally situated near Union Square subway station. The East Village is the food mecca of the universe, with many restaurants, cafes and pubs scattered throughout. Just over a block from Trader Joe's and minutes from Whole Foods and the popular Union Square Farmers Market. Tourist destinations such as the Empire State Building, Times Square, Broadway, Central Park, the World Trade Center and Williamsburg (Brooklyn) all are easily accessible.

The space
Fully furnished bedroom/office available in prewar building located on a quiet block off of 3rd Avenue in Manhattan’s famed East Village.

The apartment is roughly 750 square feet and includes a bedroom/office (the main bedroom where guests sleep), a converted bedroom, living room, eat-in kitchen and bathroom.

Note: This is a shared apartment listing. This is a converted two-bedroom apartment, which means there are two, separate, private sleeping areas. The main bedroom is the one that is available for guests. The second one is used by me.

The apartment is a 5th-floor walk-up (no elevator) and the following is included with the fee:
- High-speed Wifi throughout apartment
- Shared bathroom (includes bathtub and shower)
- Fully equipped, clean kitchen (microwave, toaster, oven, coffee machine)
-Gas, electric, heat and hot water

The larger, fully-furnished main bedroom includes:
-A full-sized loft bed (that means it's elevated from the floor)
-Large windows and a lot of sunlight (the room is 10 feet by 12 feet)
-Desk/Chair
-Comfy chair
-Pub table and stools
-Lamps
-Dresser and closet
-High-speed Wifi and HDTV
-All bed sheets, pillows, blankets, towels included

Please note: If you submit a reservation request, then that indicates that you acknowledge having read the house rules and agree to abide by them during your stay.

Any other questions...feel free to ask me.

I'd love to host your extended stay visit to exciting NYC! My private studio apartment has everything you will need for a comfortable stay. This is a true home share; you stay while I'm away🙂. My home is well kept and close to major attractions including New York-Presbyterian Hospital. Located on the Broadway strip in the Washington Heights neighborhood, you'll enjoy a convenient location close to transportation with access to parks, cafes, and bars.

The space
My studio apartment comfortably accommodates up to (2) adults.

This charming space is located inside a traditional pre-war building in uptown Manhattan. This rare gem has full door access to the fire escape. When used responsibly this is a lovely treat; let in the breeze or sit out and watch passersby.

My studio has cool tones and a big comfy queen bed! It's the perfect retreat after a long day out or hours spent at the office. The neighborhood offers many local places for dining and nights out including Hilltop Park Alehouse, Tropical Spanish Restaurant, Harlem Public, At the Wallace (Bar), Uptown Bourbon (Bar), The Honey Well (retro cocktails), Taszo Coffee Bar also serving food and wine, Semicolon (breakfast, smoothies, and coffee), The Chipped Cup (artisanal coffees and teas), ROKC (ramen + artisanal cocktails), Mama Sushi, Bo-No (Italian fare with great wine offerings), The Heights Bar & Grill, Hand-Pulled Noodles, and more - all within walking distance.

Guest access

2nd floor apartment - walkup or take the elevator. Pre-war building with everyday New Yorkers including singles, couples, and families. Onsite superintendent and laundry facilities.
