description:
Luxurious, spacious loft in prime location in Williamsburg, Brooklyn. A block from Bedford Avenue you are among the best bars, galleries, restaurants, and music in the neighborhood, and a quick 10 minute L train to Union Square. Guests enjoy sharing the space with its host, who has a busy work and travel schedule so is *rarely there* - please inquire if you have any questions.

The space
Beautiful, spacious 1,000 square foot loft on top floor, 3 blocks from Bedford Ave L stop, (2 blocks closer than Wythe Hotel). Very high (15 foot) celings, 2 skylights fill the space with sunlight along with large windows. High speed Internet. One queen-sized bed, two sleeper couches, and an available queen-sized aerobed. Two 14,000 btu A/C units will keep you frosty in case of a heatwave. Accommodates 5, living area separate from master bedroom, but not quite a 2 bedroom. Dates flexible.

Giant, 10,000 square foot roof-deck complete with furniture and spectacular views of the Manhattan Skyline, the neighborhood and the East River, complete with barbecue / grills.

Has free washer-dryer on floor, freight elevator, too many great restaurants, bars, boutiques and galleries all at your doorstep (Wythe Hotel, Brooklyn Bowl, Rough Trade, Williamsburg Music Hall). One block from new waterfront park. Three blocks to McCarren Park. 10 minutes via subway to Union Square / Manhattan all shopping, shows, events.

Possible use of vintage scooter and/or surfboards included (Rockaway Beach is 40 min away).

Thanks!

James

Other things to note
ELEVATOR - the elevator is the industrial type. As such, it's a bit more involved than a conventional elevator, but it's totally manageable and some say sort of fun.
1 - summon elevator to your floor if not already there with button on the wall next to the elevator door. You must HOLD the button until the elevator stops at your floor.
2 - after elevator arrives, open the outside doors, then raise the inside screen, and enter
3 - once you and your things are in the elevator, close the OUTSIDE doors, being careful of your hands, then lower the inside screen (the elevator will not function unless both are completely closed)
4 - now insert elevator key (very small key), turn and hold, then press your desired floor and HOLD that button as well
5 - elevator will stop at that floor then just reverse the process to get out
NOTE - please completely close BOTH screen and elevator doors after use, otherwise others cannot summon the elevator to another floor.

Additional hack if you lose the key: summon elevator, go through above steps EXCEPT do not enter the elevator; take the stairs to the desired floor and summon the elevator - you will have to use the stairs but your belongings will not : )

Garbage and recycling can be taken to the bins outside the main entrance of the building to the right of the loading dock.

listing_name: Designer’s loft, roof deck best W’burg
description:
A magnificent renovated real loft in Brooklyn ! *Please share a little about what brings you to NYC & indicate desired check in/check out times in booking request, thanks. This is a shared listing. I'm an interior designer, it's a massive loft with a large, beautiful private guest room with a dedicated workspace. The rest of the loft is shared including bathrm. 2nd bedroom (mine) is on opposite end of loft. Any questions pls ask!

The space
This 1100 sq ft listing is located in the best part of trendy Williamsburg, Brooklyn, 3 blocks from the Bedford L subway. It's on the 2nd floor of a beautifully maintained, safe loft building that was formerly a clothing factory. There are 11 ft ceilings & a lot of the unique, real loft building-type details. It's bright, unique and contemporary, with fun original artwork, lighting, and furnishings. A large yet cozy space in the heart of Williamsburg!

-Guest bedroom is a minimally, tastefully furnished room with a very comfortable (real) bed.
-Closets with drawers, shelving, and rod to hang clothing
-Large desk
-Track lights on dimmers, table lamps
-Spacious shared bathroom and living room
-Huge roof deck, views of Williamsburg Bridge and Manhattan
-Fantastic location. 5 minute walk from Bedford L, in the middle of all the neighborhood's cool and unique restaurants, bars, music venues.
-Easy street parking

More on location: When you're going somewhere for a short time, I've always felt that location definitely can make all the difference in how much you enjoy your stay. The Bedford L stop (on North 7th & Bedford Ave) is the main subway station in the heart of Williamsburg - it's one stop, or about 5 minutes away from Manhattan's East Village, and 3 stops from Union Square (the center of downtown Manhattan where most trains connect).

You can see the river when you walk out the front door. There's a beautiful park with picnic tables, outdoor concerts, an outdoor flea market & a ferry that goes back and forth to Manhattan every 20 minutes – all just a block or two away. It seems like a lot of people who come to stay in Williamsburg end up hardly ever leaving here. In short, it is pretty perfect. I look forward to answering any questions !

Guest access

listing_name: Bright and Quiet 2 BR in Park Slope
description:
This is a bright and quiet 2 bedroom apartment. It's 20 minutes away from Manhattan via the F line, just around the corner.

IMPORTANT: This apartment is not available for parties, dinner parties or any type of social gathering. No commercial bookings (photo shoots, film productions, etc.)

The space
It is a bright and quiet 2 bedroom apartment on the 3rd floor (top) of a 2-family limestone row house.

The Master bedroom is bright and quiet, with views of trees and green backyards. It has Queen size bed.

The second bedroom is smaller, with a full size bed, but faces south so it's very bright, and you see the street from the window.

The living room is bright and roomy, facing south, and is a perfect place to hang out and relax during the day or the night. The sofa converts to a sleeper sofa if needed. There is a flat screen TV with Cable TV.

The dining room is cozy and can sit up to 6 people.

The kitchen is fully equipped with oven, toaster, microwave, fridge, and all necessities, bathroom with tub and shower.

We live in the ground floor duplex, but you have a private entrance at the first floor, where there is vestibule where you can leave luggage, clothes, or even bikes!

= = = =

The house is in Park Slope, arguably the best neighborhood in New York City, at least according to New York Magazine (April 2010). It's a beautiful neighborhood with historic brownstone and limestone row houses.

Park Slope is 20 minutes away from downtown Manhattan, closer to the action than the Upper West Side or the Upper East Side. The subway line F can bring you quickly and easily anywhere in Manhattan, and the \7th Avenue\" stop is half a block away from the house.

Also half a block away is the famous Prospect Park which was designed Olmsted and Vaux

listing_name: Elegant Uptown Historic District Garden Suite
description:
Your pied-à-terre on Sugar Hill in the Jumel Terrace Historic District. Formerly a rare bookshop, the garden suite resounds with Harlem Heights' history from the Founding Fathers thru the Founding Brothers to our vibrant now. Think privacy, quiet, autonomy and a garden in bloom. Short walk, one subway stop, to NY/Columbia-Presbyterian. This is a two family house. Fully compliant with NYC short term rental laws. Hosts discretely present during your stay.

The space
Your pied-à-terre on Sugar Hill. You'll be the only guests of this owner occupied, self contained yet attached garden suite directly across the street from the Morris-Jumel Mansion, George Washington’s headquarters for 1776′s Battle of Harlem Heights and home to the infamous grand horizontal, Madame Jumel. At the time Duke Ellington dubbed the house “The Crown of Sugar Hill,” the immediate neighborhood was also home to Joe Louis, W.E.B. DuBois, Paul Robeson, Count Basie, Lena Horne, Coleman Hawkins, Andy Kirk, Don Redman, Thurgood Marshall, and dozens of other Jazz Age Masters.

The guest suite features a private entrance, full kitchen and bath (shower), a common room on the garden with single/day bed (Charles Rogers), the large center bedroom with an antique full-sized bed (arguably the quietest room in NYC), and the second, larger, front bedroom with an opulent queen size (Jessica Chastain size) 19th century French brass bed. We pride ourselves on the quality of our mattresses. All bedding has high thread counts linens, is hypo-allergenic and protected.

The full kitchen features coffee/tea facilities and welcome food supplies (fresh gourmet coffee, fresh fruit).

Rooms are furnished in a comfortable, eclectic mix with bookish comforts. There's abundant seating, two desks, two round tables, cable television, a record player, a Harlem-centric record collection, a large library on local history African & American, internet access, and a full ornamental garden with a gas grill. Wine and dine al fresco on Sunday afternoons as the sounds of Marjorie Elliot’s Parlor Jazz waft from her near by apartment.

There is housekeeping service and owners on the premises to assist you in any want or need. This is perhaps the privatest and quietest guest suite in New York City and wholly fills the most exacting legals criteria.



Enjoy local museums, entertainments and Sugar Hill cuisine, old New York charms, and easy transportation (subways and buses within two blocks, car services readily available.)

On premises: library conference rooms, parlor and garden party rentals, computer, internet service, laundry service, cable television, record & Bluetooth players.

The house’s photo shoot, conference and party facilities rates are available on inquiry.

The house has been featured in Vogue ('All That Jazz', Bruce Weber with Jessica Chastain & Omar Sy, Elle (for Gucci), Glamour (for Amanda Gorman as their \Woman of the Year\" and the video of her poem \"Learning to Love Through Loss\")

description:
Experience Brooklyn at its best in your own private and well-appointed sanctuary located on the border of Ft. Greene and Clinton Hill, two of the borough's most lively and sublime historic neighborhoods.

The space
Located on historic Clinton Avenue, one of Brooklyn's most beautiful and regal thoroughfares, this one bedroom serves as the home to a Brooklyn-based novelist and journalist. It's a superb space: clean, quiet, spacious, filled with light, and appointed in a stylish mix of midcentury and antiques that have been collected over the years. The apartment is on the 6th (top) floor of a well-maintained pre-war building (with elevator!) that has an utterly classic New York energy. A large one bedroom, it is ideal for solo travelers and couples looking to feel at home in the big city.

Guest access
When it comes to amenities, you can't ask for much more.
- High speed wifi.
- Great heat and A/C.
- Comfy queen-size mattress
- 50 inch flatscreen smart television with access to Netflix, Max, Amazon Prime, Apple TV, and all the goodies.
- Kitchen stocked with brand new, stainless steel appliances, including a dishwasher, making cleaning up after any meal a cinch.
- Given that the dining room table comfortably seats six, it's a great place to have an intimate dinner with friends.
- The bathroom has just been renovated in all white, with full-size tub and a lovely window looking out onto the neighborhood.

listing_name: Lovely 2 BR in Brooklyn's Nicest Area
description:
**Please message us first before attempting to book!**

**Three-month minimum. Guest must book entire months. No partial-month bookings. Thanks!**

Best Deal in Brooklyn: Sunny and clean 2-bedroom apartment [often used as a large 1-bedroom] w/ great Park Slope location.

Three subway lines & many cool places--restaurants, cafes, shopping--are steps away, along with banks, gyms and the post office.

No pets. No smokers. No parties. Preference for singles and couples.

The space
Everybody loves this apartment. Located on the 4th and top floor of a well-maintained small building, this 650 square foot (65 square meter) apartment gets great light and ventilation from its south and north exposures.

This is a 2-bedroom that is perfect for a single professional, couple or small family. It features nice details like wood floors, a bathroom skylight, French doors and a decorative fireplace. WiFi is fast and reliable.

There are no closets, but each bedroom has a dresser, and there is a shared bar for hanging clothes.

The building two security doors, a video intercom system, a free washer/dryer in the basement, and storage for two bicycles.

Guest access
The whole apartment is open to guests, as well as the basement laundry room.

Other things to note
The building is small and friendly. We all care very much about the building and are regularly spending money on new projects to improve it. No parties, smokers, pets or guests who are not pre-approved by us. We do not charge a cleaning fee and instead require guests to clean the apartment fully (incl sheets and towels) at the end of their stay. This is a great deal and our personal home. We prioritize guests with excellent feedback. Look forward to hearing from you!

description:
Chilled, quiet and mature environment

The space
Sunny and bright bedroom (12'x8') with direct access to garden behind it with its own bathroom. (The bathroom is NOT en suite and is NOT shared). There is a closet and TV in the room though not displayed in the pictures. description:
Live like a New Yorker! One-of-a-kind loft in an unbeatable location. The best shops, restaurants, subway access within steps. Perfect for adult families w ample areas for privacy or time together.

Quiet, spacious, tasteful, uniquely furnished 2-story apt w museum-quality art, high ceilings, private elevator.

NYC bans stays <30 days. For shorter stays, find me on the work social platform, and I will share how to legally accommodate this and Airbnb's policies. - Lisa Lefebvre/Lucid Growth Lab

The space
This 1800 sq ft / 170 sq meter apartment is situated in a historic, converted warehouse and has very high (20 ft/ 6 m) ceilings, large windows to the North and South, and a generous skylight, all of which make the apartment sunny -- a rarity in Manhattan.

The space has a rotating art collection. Some paintings and photographs were made by friends, some by artists who had retrospectives at MoMA, and others from contemporary art galleries in South Africa where I used to travel for work.

TO REQUEST A BOOKING
Because this is my home, please tell me a bit about who will be staying. Given that this is an upscale, small, quiet building, the loft is best suited for mature, respectful adults. Note that quiet hours are 8 am - 11 pm, and no parties are allowed.

LOCATION
If you are browsing this listing, you already know SoHo is the most stimulating, creative, and centrally located NYC neighborhood to live in, work in, and visit. Many celebrities live in this area. Even if you don't book this particular loft, I highly recommend visitors stay in SoHo.

SoHo is at the center of the most interesting neighborhoods in NYC, almost all of which are within walking distance: NoLiTa, Greenwich Village, Union Square, Flatiron, Chinatown, East Village/Lower East Side, and TriBeCa.

DOWNSTAIRS LAYOUT
On the first floor, the keyed elevator opens up into a large living and dining area. A full bath is off to the left. It is small, but clean and functional, with a shower and shelving for toiletries.

The kitchen is fully stocked with tools and pantry items which you are welcome to use if needed. There is a pharmaceutical-grade water filter under the kitchen sink.

In the back of the first floor is a cozy living room situated under the large skylight -- this is the room most favored by my friends.

UPSTAIRS LAYOUT
The second floor has an additional sitting area with a television. If needed, the couch can be converted into a comfortable single bed to sleep 5 in total. *Please let me know if you would like this option before your arrival.*

The upstairs bathroom includes double sinks, a sauna, a jacuzzi tub, and a shower stall. There is a pharmaceutical-grade water filter on the upstairs shower head. This means the water pressure is a little lower than normal, but all toxins will have been filtered out.

There is also a full washer/dryer, iron, and ironing board.

SLEEPING AREAS
There are two sleeping areas upstairs. Mattresses are exceptionally comfortable with memory foam toppers. All linens are hotel-quality, and used only for guests.

The front sleeping area is an alcove with a Double bed. This room is situated under the skylight. Because this is a loft, it does not have a door but there is an opaque curtain that can be drawn shut for privacy. (It is wonderful to sleep here during a rain or snow storm!)

The back sleeping area has a Queen bed and a wall of windows. It is very quiet. The back bedroom windows can include blackout curtains. *Please mention you would like this option when booking.*

Please note this is a true loft so there are no bedroom doors, but both sleeping areas are very private from each other. The floor plan in the photo gallery section can help you understand the layout of the bedrooms vis-à-vis each other.

While there are no closets per se, there is a garment rack with ample hangers for hanging clothes, suitcase racks, and a wardrobe in the front/library bedroom.

A CHALLENGE TO SOME, AN ADVANTAGE TO OTHERS ...
The loft has many artful, delicate objects and furnishings. A few guests have commented that this is stressful for families with small children. If you would like me to remove fragile objects for your stay, please let me know as I would be glad to do this.

TRANSPORTATION
SoHo is the only neighborhood in NYC where all 5 major Manhattan subways have stops, so it is an extremely convenient and fast area from which to get around Manhattan or to Brooklyn.

INSIDER EXPERIENCES
Please see the Airbnb app for a neighborhood guide with insider tips on what to do in the neighborhood and beyond. Someone could literally spend a month in the 20 square blocks surrounding the apartment and still not fully experience all the interesting options in the area.

I look forward to the possibility of being your host and helping you enjoy this wonderful neighborhood!

CHECK IN/CHECK OUT TIMES
Check-in time is 4:00 PM, and check-out time is 11 AM.

ADDITIONAL CLEANING
I have a wonderful housekeeper who can help with weekly bedding/towel laundry and cleaning for a pass-through cost of $200. *Please let me know when booking if you would like this service*.

IN SUM
I look forward to the possibility of hosting you and helping enjoy NYC like a true New Yorker.

Guest access
The entire loft.

Other things to note
There are a few reasonable house rules: Quiet hours are 8 am - 11 pm; no parties; no smoking; kindly remove shoes when in the apartment; please return apartment and kitchen to the state you found it in before checking out; kindly follow recycling guidelines; and turn off lights/AC when not in apartment (apartment runs on expensive wind power).

listing_name: 1 Bed 1 Bath Apartment Heart of Union Square
description:
The space
This 450 sf apartment is in the heart of the NYC action! Easy walk to all subway stops, world class restaurants and shopping. This is a completely private apartment. You do not share the kitchen or the bath with anyone else.
On tree lined quiet quaint 17th st and Irving Place. Apartment is a 3rd floor walk up and comes complete with everything you need! A comfy queen size bed in bedroom, a new West Elm convertible sofa, AC, microwave, dishes, your own kitchen (not shared), new towels, new linens, flat screen TV, Roku player w Amazon Instant Video, Netflix and High speed internet (wifi) etc. I dont have traditional cable but you get Roku channels.
Apartment is next to almost all major subway lines as Union Square is a major hub. Also, you are a 5 minute walk from the famous Union Square Farmers Market and Whole Foods, 2 streets over from NYU, 3 min walk to a great gym, yoga, spin class, and a stone's throw from some of the city's finest restaurants.
Laundry is just down the street.
The place is unique in that it really feels like a New York apartment. The pre war character and the exposed brick make this a true hidden gem in Manhattan. Because it is a prewar building the heat in the winter can be too hot. If you are not comfortable opening the window a bit or turning the fan from the outside on until the building regulates then thismay not be the place for you. The bedroom gets a ton of sunlight and looks out onto a beautiful neighborhood street. Get the real New York experience and stay in the heart of it all!
Nearby attractions include the mysterious and historic Gramercy Park which you can walk around but only members have a key, music shows at Irving Plaza, an off Broadway spectacle at the Union Square or Daryl Roth Theater, people watching in Union Square Park and of course tons of great food and lounges.

Please make sure that your Airbnb profile is complete, a photo and ID are included.

This is a non smoking rental.

This is a private apartment. It is not shared with anyone else.

Check in time is 4pm at the earliest. You can check in 24 hours per day
Check out time is 12PM at the latest.You can check out earlier however
This is a strict policy! I cannot make exceptions. However there is a web service called Vertoe that will locate and reserve a nearby luggage storage for your convenience

Guest access
You have access to the entire apartment. This includes a private bathroom, and a private kitchen.

Other things to note
Keys are located in a nearby \Key Cafe\" You will receive instructions before you arrive on setting up your account to access the keys."

listing_name: Large private suite in our home in Brooklyn.
description:
Beautiful, restful, and spacious PRIVATE space. We, the hosts, live here, but the space is designed for total privacy. What's pictured is your private space during your stay.
Away from the hustle and bustle of the city, yet still close to it.
Please ASK for availability by messaging the host option before placing a direct reservation request. You can do this by clicking on my image in the listing page instead of the reserve button.

The space
NOTE that this is a private home and we live in the home. You have access to everything that you see in the listing's pictures as your space.
While we adhere to NYC laws, which mandate that the space be shared, we make sure that our guests feel that their privacy is fully respected at all times and have designed the space accordingly.

This grand, spacious floor-through is in a classic 1890s brownstone house in the historic Bedford-Stuyvesant neighborhood of Brooklyn. Located on a quiet, tree-lined street, 25/30 minutes from and to Downtown Manhattan by subway, door to door. Walk to bars, restaurants & galleries in Bushwick & Stuyvesant Heights. A short subway ride to Williamsburg, and 1 block from a supermarket, liquor store & laundromat.

Neighborhood:
Originally built for the Victorian upper-middle class in the 1800s, predominantly Dutch & other European immigrants. In the 1930s, the neighborhood changed socially & economically, like many other areas in New York City. Until the late 1980's it was mostly African American, during which it developed its own cultural history. Many famous jazz musicians, actors, rappers & politicians came from here. Now in the 2010s, it is an incredible mix of races with varied socio-economic backgrounds. Many of the old-time residents still live here. More recently, there has been an influx of more diverse younger residents, attracted to the neighborhood for the beautiful architecture and the convenience of Manhattan.

Transportation:
Subways are the J, Z and M trains to Myrtle Avenue/Broadway station, or J and Z to Kosciuszko Street station. Both stations are parallel to the house and are about a 7 to 10-minute walk at a moderate pace. (7 short blocks to Myrtle, or 2 city blocks to Kosciuszko, same distance). About 25 minutes door to-door to Lower Manhattan. Also accessible to the G train, to Bedford/Nostrand station (5 city blocks, about a 15-minute walk). Very reliable and steady buses as well (B15, B38 & B52) for travel inside Brooklyn. You can do a search engine on New York's MTA transportation system so that you get a sense of how connected the space is. The best airports for the location of the house are JFK and La Guardia. Even though it is reachable, Newark International Airport is discouraged due to the distance and/or complicated public transportation connections. About a $35.00 cab/car ride from JFK / La Guardia airports. There is Public transportation (subway) from JFK Airport to the neighborhood's subway stations. La Guardia is reachable by car/taxi only.

Accommodations:
This is a private space. The main room has a dining table & a living room/sitting area. The bedroom features a 'queen' size' bed for two, it is a real bed with a box spring and mattress. The bedroom also has a vanity table with a mirror and a bookcase with a desk. There are two closets & a chest of drawers in the bathroom hallway. The bathroom has a tub with a shower, tile walls, and a ceramic floor. The original architectural details, high ceilings, and hardwood floors give it a real 'Brooklyn brownstone' feel & charm. The space is heated during the winter with central heating, and there is an air conditioner installed in the bedroom during the summer.

The space has Wi-Fi, and multiple power outlets for computers, cell phones & personal electronics. There is a TV connected to a casting router that allows access to YouTube and Netflix. A DVD player in the apartment. The TV can be connected to some laptops and used as a monitor if yours has an HDMI port. Or you could also get an HDMI-compatible connecting cable to your computer. No cable TV or TV reception though.

Private bedroom
Private bathroom
Dedicated kitchen area.

This is a smoke- and pet-free environment for your comfort as well as the comfort of the following guests.

There are cameras at the main entrance for security reasons.

Any questions, please ask. I'm more than happy to answer, and my response rate is very fast. Before officially placing a reservation request, ask to make sure there are no calendar conflicts.

description:
Sunny room in a cute & chic Manhattan pied-a-terre located on a quiet street on the Upper West Side of Manhattan. Central Park is right outside the front door, as is easy access to all major subway & bus lines. Enjoy city living, culture & country comfort!

The space
Students & wanderers are warmly welcomed! Cute and chic fully furnished 1 bedroom in a 2 bedroom and 1 bathroom apartment on the Upper West Side of Manhattan btwn Columbus Ave & Central Park West. Situated 3 blocks from Riverside Park, 2 blocks from Morningside Park and a 1 block hop, skip and jump from Central Park. Located in the Columbia University area and conveniently near public transportation - steps away from major subway and bus lines. Cafes, restaurants, bars, delis, grocery stores (along Broadway), cultural offerings (St. John the Divine, American Museum of Natural History, El Museo del Barrio, Lincoln Center for the Performing Arts, The Apollo Theater, Studio Museum in Harlem, Metropolitan Museum, etc) and a Farmer's Market on weekends (in Morningside Park) are all nearby.

The apartment is on the 5th floor of a very safe & secure (camera & private code secured entry) building with an elevator. The fully equipped galley-style kitchen includes a gas stove & oven, microwave and coffee maker. The guest bedroom is private and has a double bed, which can accommodate 2ppl who are traveling/sleeping together. The bathroom has a full tub and shower.

The B/C subway line is one block away on Central Park West and the 1/9 subway line is 3 blocks away on Broadway and 110th Street. And, several public buses stop directly in front of the apartment.

Amenities incl: High-speed internet WiFi & LED HD Samsung Smart TV with free Netflix included. Linens, blankets, kitchen appliances, tableware and cleaning materials are also provided.

Guest access
Guests have access to the living area, bathroom and kitchen.

During your stay
Shared space with privacy, as per guest preferences.

Ideally, guests are independent and savvy travelers. As such, I am prepared to share my home with hospitality and privacy.

Other things to note
Given these uncertain times, I wanted to provide potential guests with an update in response to the recent pandemic.

As the situation around coronavirus (COVID-19) continues to evolve, please know that I am strictly following Airbnb's guidance and doing everything that I can to ensure my guest's best welfare.

And, as always, the safety and security of my guests remain my highest priority. I take great pride as a Superhost in maintaining high standards of cleanliness and hygiene. In response to the coronavirus (COVID-19), I have made sure to take extra precautionary and proactive steps in cleaning and disinfecting my home. Rest assured, I am being much more rigorous than I typically am noted to be (see guest reviews). In addition, the building maintenance staff has adjusted their cleaning and have ramped up building-wide efforts to sanitize and disinfect the public areas.

If you have any further questions about my home and the preparedness measures that I have instituted during this challenging time, please feel free to ask.


I am happy to welcome and accommodate business travelers, interns, university students, professors and staff who may have extended stay needs. Please inquire about longer term stay specials.

description:
Refurnished in 2024. This parlor floor apartment in a historic Brooklyn brownstone provides a sunny breakfast space in which to begin the day, and affords relaxed dining and wind-down at the end of the day in an environment that is elegant, spacious, and warm. Bedrooms are away from the street and very quiet. Excellent restaurants can be found close-by for those who wish to eat out. The neighborhood is calm and retains a deep-rooted and welcoming community.

The space
* English-language description is followed by write-up in French & Spanish
* Un texte français suit celui en anglais
* Una descripción en español sigue las otras

NOTE: We took some time in 2024 to replace certain furniture, rugs, lighting, and appliances so as to keep the place sharp. We also had the brownstone façade replaced -- and the result is gorgeous!

THE LODGING
Gorgeous two-bedroom parlor floor apartment with 10-ft ceilings in a renovated and restored 1800’s brownstone on a quiet block of historic Stuyvesant Heights. The apartment is very private and occupies the entire parlor floor of the house. It offers a grand living room with original carved wooden window trimming and the original parquet floor beautifully preserved; a complete kitchen with plenty of cabinet space; and two bedrooms equipped collectively with three full-sized beds – an individual bed in one room, and a bunkbed in the other. The bathroom is equipped with a tub and shower and generous cabinet space. Closet space is ample, augmented with plentiful overhead suitcase storage space. The place is fully furnished with tables, comfortable seating, and good lighting. Kitchen is stocked with coffee maker, toaster oven, microwave, vegetable chopper, full refrigerator and freezer, coffee, sugar, salt, spices, and basic cooking and serving essentials. Free wireless internet connection available. Small multi-lingual library provided. Tall living room windows face south, flooding the space with sunlight in the winter. Shades on the windows can be drawn for privacy, and to help keep the space cool in the summer (which is easy, given the high ceilings and the cross-draft).

OCCUPANCY
Four-person maximum.
Children twelve years or older welcome.
No pets.

PROVISIONS
clean bed linens, pillows, blankets, and towels
gas stove with oven
coffee maker, toaster oven, microwave, vegetable chopper, teapot with wire filtration basket
coffee, tea, sugar, salt, spices, etc
iron and ironing board
hair dryer on request
free wireless internet
guide to local restaurants, shops, and services (including my recommendations and those of previous guests)
subway map and Brooklyn map

CLEAN AIR POLICY
No smoking in the apartment or anywhere in the building or yard -- including electronic cigarettes. No incense.

THE HOST
On hand or easily reachable and ready to assist in case anything is needed.

THE NEIGHBORHOOD
Stuyvesant Heights is the elegant historic district of Bedford-Stuyvesant, a neighborhood that has produced a host of well-known jazz musicians, rappers, poets, and other artists – but where you might also hear a clarinetist practicing late Brahms chamber music. The population today mixes multi-generation families deeply rooted in the old community network with an influx of younger folk newly discovering the charms of the lovely tree-lined streets, the beautiful architecture, and the dignified manners of neighbors who gracefully welcome new arrivals. A neighborhood tour is staged each fall to show off the lavish elegance and artistry preserved in many of the grand old houses.

SERVICES
Restaurants abound, including the popular Saraghina (one minute walk from the house), which is a classy, atmospheric, yet well priced venue for pizza and other Italian cuisine, drawing people from all over the city; Celestino, specializing in Mediterranean seafood dishes; Peaches, a lively place much frequented by a loyal local crowd and serving up regional American classics; Peaches Hothouse, reputed for its hot pepper chicken; and Besos, featuring Latino-inspired dishes. Within a two-minute walk, you can also find a wine bar, a health-food store, a twenty-four hour deli with fresh produce and organic eggs and sandwich essentials, a drop-off/pick-up laundromat, and the local branch of the New York Public Library. For those looking for gospel music, there is no reason to run all the way to Harlem: a three-minute walk from our doorstep will bring you to a beautiful old church that will welcome you warmly and offer plenty of good singing. Another church lightly chimes the hour in the background, helping you keep track of the rhythm of the day.

FRESH DAILY BREAD
We are pleased to announce the arrival of a bakery, one minute from our doorstep, offering fresh baguettes and other loaves daily, baked on the premises.

THE TRAINS
Eight-minute walk (depending on your stride) down elegant Stuyvesant Avenue (an increasingly frequent location for big film shoots) to a station where both the A-express and C-local trains are available. Trains provide easy access to Manhattan (10-15 minutes to lower Manhattan once on the train, half an hour to Penn Station; 40 minutes to Central Park); and, more locally, to Brooklyn Heights, Prospect Park, the Brooklyn Botanic Garden, and the Brooklyn Museum. In the other direction, you can get to the Air Train to JFK Airport, about a half-hour from our station. Add another fifteen minutes to arrive at the beaches at Far Rockaway.

On the local C-train, it will be just ten minutes to Fort Greene, home of the Brooklyn Academy of Music (excellent venue for film, dance, theater, and concerts), Mark Morris Dance Studios, and myriad diverse restaurants, bars, and late-night spots.

LANGUAGES FOR CORRESPONDENCE
E-mails are accepted in English, French, and Spanish.

ADDITIONAL POSITIVE POINTS
In addition to what is described above, we offer:
Thick firm mattresses – no back-ache in the morning -- full-size: 54 inches wide
calm neighbors: no one will bombard you with excessive noise or drama; the people above you do not wear shoes inside
excellent privacy: main entrance serves only two apartments; no one passes your door; the magnolia tree in the front yard shields the living room from the street with its curtain of leaves
quick assistance: either myself or my housemate is easily accessible in case any questions or issues arise
cleanliness: no rodents have signed leases here, and they generally seem to respect the law

==============================

EN FRANÇAIS

UN APPARTEMENT À DEUX PERSONNES TRANQUILLE ET ENSOLEILLÉ DANS LE QUARTIER HISTORIQUE DE BEDFORD-STUYVESANT

L’appartement se situe dans l’étage le plus elegant d’une maison traditionelle de Brooklyn, un “brownstone” comme on dit, construite dans le 19ème siècle. Vu son âge, j’ai fait une rénovation de toute la maison en 2005, en préservant ce qui restait des détails historiques, et, peut–être plus important, en améliorant les systèmes d’électricité et de plombier. L’appartement qu’on offre aux voyageurs est bien reculé et consiste d’une grande salle de séjour à haut plafond dont les fenêtres donnent sur le rideau de feuilles d’un arbre qui cache l’appartement de la rue; une cuisine bien équipée où on peut facilement preparer un diner pour quatre personnes; une salle de bain simple et propre avec une douche et une baignoire; et deux chambres à dormir où se dressent trois lits très confortables.

L’escalier qui mène à cet espace sert à deux appartements – celui réservé aux voyageurs et l’autre en bas où vivent deux locataires très gentilles et calmes. Donc la maison est douée d’une tranquilité confortable et on peut facilement mener ici une vie privée. Dans le but de préserver cette tranquilité pour nous tous, qui travaillent selon des horaires individuels et diverses, je demande aux voyageurs de se comporter d’une manière calme et respectueuse pour que personne ne risque de perdre le sommeil qui est tellement précieux à New York. Si vous voudriez rencontrer des amis, je vous demande de les rencontrer dans un café ou un restaurant (il y en a assez dans ce quartier où on peut tarder comme on veuille). Même plus important, pour le bonheur et la santé de toute la populace de la maison, et aussi pour le bonheur des voyageurs qui nous visiteront après vous, je vous exige absolument de ne pas fumer dans aucune chambre de l’appartement, même par les fenêtres ouvertes, même dans le jardin près de l’entrée. Dit ça, si vous imaginez que vous trouverez également l’appartement et les conditions de vivre ici bien agréables, c’est sûr que vous vous trouverez très confortable et bienvenu chez nous.

Le quartier lui-même est tranquille et présente une jolie architecture. Beaucoup de musiciens de jazz et de rap, et d’autres artistes, ont commencé leur carrière ici. Aujourd’hui la populace est un mélange composé de familles qui habitent ici depuis de générations et les jeunes qui arrivent actuellement, attirés par la beauté des maisons et des rues avec ses arbres et fleurs bien soignées, et attirés aussi par les bonnes manières et le bienvenu offerts par la vieille communauté. On trouve très près de la maison une agréable variété de restaurants, de cafés, et de magasins pour acheter la nourriture. Pour ceux qui cherchent écouter de la musique “gospel,” vous pouvez l’encontrer ici, trois minutes à pied dès notre maison. Si vous voudriez aller à Manhattan, les trains vous y ammeneront dans une demi-heure.

Si vous avez des questions sur l’appartement ou le quartier, tout le plaisir est à nous de vous donner des réponses les plus utiles que soit possible.

Merci beaucoup pour l’interêt porté sur notre appartement; bonne route; et à bientôt!

==============================

EN ESPAÑOL

UN REFUGIO SOLEADO DONDE SE PUEDE RELAJAR O DORMIR TRANQUILAMENTE EN LA CIUDAD QUE NO DUERME NUNCA

IMPORTANTE
Algunos de los habitantes que viven en este edificio son muy sensibles al humo. Pues se prohibe de fumar en el piso y en toda parte de la casa. Gracias por ayudarnos en el objetivo de bien cuidar de todos que se encuentran debajo nuestro techo. (Para que sepa usted, nadie va a gritarle si quema el tostado...)

EL APARTAMENTO
El apartamento que alquilamos se encuentra en el segundo piso de una casa del siglo 19 que renové completamente en 2005. Hay dos cuartos que contienen tres camas; una sala brillante con dos ventanas grandes que dan al sur. Esta es dividida en dos espacios: uno equipado de una mesa para cenar y el otro que contiene dos sillones comodos y un canapé; una cocina complete donde se pueden encontrar el café, el té, el azucar, y varias especias; y un baño sencillo y limpio. Uno de los cuartos es equipado de un escritorio elegante donde se puede leer o escribir a la intimidad. Este piso es muy tranquilo: aunque esté usted en New York, muy cercano a Manhattan, podra dormir aquí placidamente.

NUMERO MAXIMO DE HABITANTES
Cuatro personas. Por favor, no animales.

APROVISIONAMIENTOS
Sábanas, mantas, y toallas limpias
Hornillo con cuatro quemadores, tostadora, cafetera, micro-onda
Café, té, azucar, sal, especias...
Wifi internet gratis
Guía de restaurantes y tiendas
Mapa de trenes & de Brooklyn

EL DUEÑO DE LA CASA
Vivo y de veces trabajo en el piso por encima del cual alquilamos. Pues no es difícil encontrarme si hay preguntas. Si falta alguna cosa, hay que decírmelo, y estaré contento a ayudar. Note que evitamos de llevar zapatos en nuestro apartamento, pues no va usted a soñar con elefantes que bailan toda la noche.

EL BARRIO
Stuyvesant Heights es un barrio histórico y residencial, mostrando la arquitectura característica y elegante de las casas de Brooklyn del decimonoveno siglo – los “brownstones”, como se llaman. Nacieron y trabajaban aquí muchos músicos de jazz y rap, y otros artistas y escritores. Hoy día la población es una mezcla entre las familias establecidas aquí por generaciones y los jóvenes de otros barrios, ciudades, y países que han recientemente descubierto la belleza, la tranquilidad, y los arboles viejos y magníficos del barrio. Se puede encontrar muy cerca de la casa una diversidad de restaurantes – ofriendo la comida italiana, mediterranea, americana latina, etc – y almacenes que pueden proveer frutas, carnes, quesos, sabones y otras necesidades. También hay lavanderías no muy lejos.

LOS TRENES
Estamos cerca del tren que viene del aeropuerto JFK y que continúa para Manhattan. Es posible salir de la casa y llegar por tren a Mahattan en unos treinta minutos.

¿PREGUNTAS?
Si usted tiene cualquier pregunta, le pedimos que nos contacte. Gracias por su interés.

Guest access
Guests have access to the entire apartment, which is all for their private use.

Other things to note
This is a very quiet house at night. In the bedrooms, you might even forget that you are in a big city. One advantage you will find here in terms of getting a good night’s sleep is that each bedroom has an insulated dropped ceiling, so that the light, respectful footsteps that we may make over your heads in the evening will be of little consequence. Then, at midnight plus or minus, we retreat to an upper floor, so that you will have no one directly overhead for most of the night. That makes for a very peaceful space.

listing_name: Sunny, Newly Redecorated Apt in Hip Brooklyn Area
description:
You’ll be staying in one of Brooklyn’s most fun and popular neighborhoods of Prospect Heights, a short walk from several bars, restaurants and boutiques! My place is a modern apartment with lots of sunlight, air-conditioning and plenty of space.

The building is fairly quiet and occupied by mostly young professionals and new families.

- 20 min ride to lower Manhattan/PATH train
- 30-45 min ride to Midtown/Rockefeller
- Short walk to 2/3/4/5/S trains
- 15-20 min walk to Prospect Park

The space
Elevator building
Tons of sunlight
Warm all year long (Note: the apartment stays pretty warm due to sunlight and building heat during Winter. Air conditioning available in bedroom)
Mid-century modern + cultural decor
Open concept kitchen & living room w/ huge windows
Gas stove & microwave
Large spacious bedroom
Small bar-stool desk for working
Queen size bed
Large leather couch for 3rd person
Bright bathroom with 2 mirrors
Fresh linens, towels and washcloths
Apple TV + Roku (no cable)

ABOUT THE NEIGHBORHOOD
Prospect Heights is one of the fastest growing, hottest neighborhoods in Brooklyn. Before you even leave the block you can enjoy some of the best tacos you'll ever have at our local Mexican restaurant, and a right turn up the street offers 3 different coffee houses, 4 bars, 2 organic grocery stores, a yoga studio, more restaurants, boutiques and a host of other attractions to explore during your free time. The people in the neighborhood are mostly young professionals, some with children, and it truly feels like a community.

The building is across the street from a train (literally you can see it from the living room window) that connects to 7 different train lines to Manhattan! Very convenient and you can get back home easily from almost anywhere in the city.

• Walking distance to Prospect Park, Brooklyn Botanical Garden, Brooklyn Museum
• 1 stop from Target, Atlantic Station, Long Island Railroad & Barclay's Center on 4,5 train
• 24 hour laundromat a block away
• Full grocery store around the corner (same block as bldg)

GETTING AROUND
Walking distance to S, 2,3,4,5 trains
Shuttle (S) train connections to C, B, Q trains
Local taxis come in about 5 minutes usually and go to both airports (JFK & LGA)

SUBWAY RIDES (times are approximate; based on express train service)
5 minutes to Barclay's Center
20 minutes to lower Manhattan
30 minutes to Union Square
35 minutes to Times Square
40 minutes to Columbus Circle/Central Park

Check in: 3 PM
Check out: 12 PM
(These times are negotiable, just please let me know in advance)

Guest access
Living room, bedroom, kitchen & bathroom.

Other things to note
The building regulates the temperature and it’s toasty during winter! If you’re hot feel free to open windows or use the A/C in the bedroom to cool off.

description:
Bright, spacious, and clean private room, perfect for a short stop or a longer stay in one of Brooklyn’s hottest neighborhoods. The location could not be better; with the L-train half a block from the Montrose Ave. stop, taking you to union sq. in 15min (counting the time to get to the station), CitiBikes on the corner, amazing supermarket next block and awesome restaurants, bars, cafes and bakeries within a couple of blocks.

The space
About the space:

3 near identical private rooms, roughly 150 sf. each: One would be yours.

1 shared bathroom.

Common space for eating and socializing.

The 3 private rooms have a large window facing east, giving the room a magical quality at and after dawn. Customize the amount of light in the room with blinds and blackout rollers to suit your preferences. You’ll have a full-size bed with either a winter duvet or summer light sheet, two pillows, two nightstands and lamps. You’ll have a small desk, a clothes hanger and always eventful views onto Bushwick Ave.

The bathroom you would share with the inhabitant(s) of the neighboring rooms so long hot showers and major grooming would not be so welcome when other guests are around. However, if no other guests are around you will be able to take a very hot shower for a long time. In the bathroom you’ll find shampoo, conditioner, body soap, extra toilet paper, q-tips, hair dryer, first aid kit, pain killers etc.

The common space is a very cozy space where you can do very basic cooking and eat and drink. It’s a lovely space to have coffee or tea in the morning but of course be mindful of your neighbors so smooth movements in the early and late hours please.

There is a laundry room on the 7th floor of the building and amazing views of the city on the roof deck which I absolutely recommend you check out.

During your stay
My apartment is in adjacent space that is connected to the airbnb portion you'll be staying in. I like to meet all my guests if I can, just because it makes hosting so much more enjoyable :) However, you probably won't see much of me but I'm always around if any issues arise or if you have some questions :)

description:
You are WELCOME to inquire for STAYS LESS THAN 30 days as well (i.e. in June 2025 for instance)

A gorgeous brand new and charming loft style 1BR apartment in New York City's most desired SoHo neighborhood. Across the street from the famous former artist lofts of Andy Warhol and Jean Basquiat!

The space
Tastefully decorated with all new furniture. This is a top floor sun drenched apartment with double exposure windows that provide an abundance of natural light giving the apartment an open airy feeling. 6 large windows offer spectacular views of SoHo's famous cast iron district. Spacious kitchen with high end stainless steel appliances, granite countertop, and wooden cabinets. Windowed bathroom with a tiled shower and pedestal sink.

A gorgeous brand new and charming loft style 1BR apartment in New York City's most desired SoHo neighborhood. Tastefully decorated with all new furniture. This is a top floor sun drenched apartment with double exposure windows that provide an abundance of natural light giving the apartment an open airy feeling. 6 large windows offer spectacular views of SoHo's famous cast iron district. Spacious kitchen with high end stainless steel appliances, granite countertop, and wooden cabinets. Windowed bathroom with a tiled shower and pedestal sink.

The apartment is situated arguably on the most sought after block in NYC's historic and fashionable SoHo neighborhood on lovely Prince street! Literally steps away from the city's best galleries, cafes, restaurants, boutiques, clubs, street art vendors and perhaps the worlds finest shopping mile. In the heart of SoHo, it is a walking distance to the West Village, Greenwich Village, Tribeca, NoHo, NoLita, Little Italy, NYU, Chinatown and the East Village. Numerous delis as well as a Whole Foods Organic market are all within walking distance. Weekly farmer's market on Union Square, which is only 5min away on the subway. A brand new Trader Joe's just opened as well literally a 4min. walk away!

The apartment has been newly renovated and has a brand new convertible designer sofa, a new desk an chairs in the living room and a new full size mattress in the separate bedroom. We also got a new organic tatami mat as a support foundation for the mattress and mounted new blinds in the bedroom (most of it is not yet visible on the photos). The apartment is on the top floor and there is no elevator. The location, the astonishing views and quietness of the apartment make for a perfect stay in the heart of downtown Manhattan. 2–5mins away from all major subway lines (1/A/B/C/D/E/F/R and N). Also, right in front of the house is a new city bike station where you can pick up a bike and ride through the city and drop it off again at any city bike station in NYC. Enjoy a true and authentic New York experience in the middle of it all yet in a quiet oasis once you come home from all your adventures. SoHo is New York's safest neighborhood! Feel free to message me for any additional info/questions.


**UPDATE – PLEASE NOTE***

Additional info: we just fully renovated the apartment! We purchased a new organic mat foundation as a support for the mattress so the bed is off the floor, as well as a new luxury memory foam mattress (January 2025). A new designer sofa as well as a new table for the living room and bamboo Roman shades for the bedroom just arrived. New decorations, art books, and dishes have also been purchased. The apartment has just been painted and looks a lot nicer with higher end furniture!

listing_name: Room w/pvt bathroom on Central Park
description:
The beautiful guest bedroom w/private black marble bathroom is fully furnished in my renovated 3BR apartment in Manhattan on Upper West Side; across the street is Central Park! This classic turn-of-the century pre-war building is also close to subway, buses, museums & more! I'm a native NY'er, Airbnb host since 2012 & Super Host!

[ NOTE: Due to new NYC regulations for short term stays as of Sept 1, 2023, we are only accepting bookings with stays over 30 days. Please msg us if interested. ]

The space
Hello! I have a dedicated, beautiful guest bedroom with a private black marble bathroom, in my apartment in Manhattan on the Upper West Side. This classic New York building is turn-of-the century (pre-war / c. 1912), on the corner of Central Park West... across the street is Central Park! I'm a native NY'er, Airbnb host since 2012 & Super Host!

The apartment was fully renovated before I moved in (2004) and had it re-painted in 2020 and 2024. Please note, the apartment is a 4th floor walkup (no elevator).

The apartment is conveniently located near subway and buses... within walking distance to over a dozen museums, and you can get almost anywhere in Manhattan within 20-40 minutes, door-to door!

* Since the question comes up a lot, I am only renting this bedroom, not the entire apartment (I live in one of the bedrooms!). Also, please do not rent the room because of the piano... this is not a practice studio or a concert instrument. Guests may use the piano occasionally but it's not for hours of practice a day.

[ NOTE: Due to new NYC regulations for short term stays starting Sept 1, 2023, we can only accept bookings with stays over 30 days. We also had to move our listing, so reviews post pandemic don't show up here, unfortunately. Please msg us if interested or any questions. ]


THE ROOM:
This beautiful room is a dedicated guest bedroom, and is fully furnished with a private black marble full-bathroom (only you will use this bathroom!). There is beautiful brick wall, dark hardwood floors, and a wall-unit that lets you hang clothes, with drawers and shelves so you can fully unpack - you don't have to live out of a suitcase. There is a plush pillow-top Kings Down full-size mattress on a captains bed with 2 drawers that is available to put more of your things in. There is also a small wooden desk and chair. This is an interior bedroom within the apartment, and the windows face a light well (not Central Park). There is high-speed Wi-Fi throughout apartment, including this bedroom, and you are free to use it.

I will supply sheets and towels. The room will comfortably fit a couple. The bed has a cozy down comforter, and pillows. You are welcome to hang out in the large and comfortable living room during your stay. Guests are welcome to use the kitchen as much (or little) as you want. There is a shelf in the refrigerator for your food, as well as space in the cabinets to put groceries. I don't provide any food for guests, but there are grocery stores nearby. I have spices, sugar, etc which am happy to share.


THE APARTMENT
This is a large, furnished, 3 bedroom apartment with 2 marble bathrooms & kitchen (approx 1200 sq ft). It is a beautifully renovated pre-war building, and we are on the 4th floor (walk-up). There is hi speed wi-fi (mesh network) in the apartment. We live in the other two bedrooms so cannot rent the other rooms.


Most guests say this apartment looks like the quintessential New York City apartment, being right on Central Park with the brick walls in every room, fire escape, grand piano, and dark wood floors.


Since I am a composer and music producer the apartment is filled with musical things... photos and posters from my shows, musical instruments, and my studio setup. I also have a 6ft baby-grand Baldwin piano which guests can play during the day, when myself and my roommate are out of the apartment. I've had many wonderful musical guests stay, ranging from students auditioning at music conservatory to musicians performing at Carnegie Hall!


THE NEIGHBORHOOD
The neighborhood is residential area that is quiet and safe! The Police and Fire station is 2 blocks away. We are also within 2 blocks from a lot of stores including Whole Foods, a wine store, several pharmacies, various stores, banks, a bakery, several restaurants, and dry cleaner. 3 blocks away on Broadway are tons of restaurants, and many deliver.


THE LOCATION
We are located right on Central Park West on the Upper West Side of Manhattan - a very residential area. You can get almost anywhere in Manhattan within 20-40 minutes. Within walking distance of the apartment are more then a dozen museums including the Metropolitan Museum of Art, Guggenheim, Natural History Museum, Hayden Planetarium, Neue Gallery, Jewish Museum, and many more. Also within walking distance is the famous \Tom's Diner\"used in Seinfeld!


TRANSPORTATION
The 96th street B and C subway is a 2 minute walk from the building. Within 20 minutes (by subway) you can be in Times Square and the theater district for Broadway Shows. Within 20 to 40 min you can be almost anywhere in Manhattan! You can walk thru the park in about 15 or 20 minutes to the Metropolitan Museum of Art
room_type:
or the Natural History Museum. From any of the main airports it will take about 1.5hrs to Newark or JFK by public transportation and 45-60 min by taxi. To Laguardia about 60 min by public transportation and 25-30 min by taxi.

description:
Fully furnished 1-bedroom ground floor apartment with a private back garden in a happening Brooklyn neighborhood of restaurants, bars & shopping. It's a typical small Brooklyn home from the 1860s! and was renovated with eco-attention and furnished with class and all the comforts.

The space
Really nice 1-bedroom floor through with a private back garden in a hip Brooklyn neighborhood. Our house is quiet and serene. The apartment will be empty for at least 3 days before your arrival. Thorough cleaning and disinfection of all surfaces is guaranteed. Your safety is our top concern.

Please note that we keep a shoe-free house. You should leave your outside shoes in the wood bench for that purpose outside of your front door. Please bring house shoes or slippers with you for use inside the apartment. Thanks for helping to keep the space super-clean!

Apartment has hardwood bamboo floors, a ceramic tile bathroom, granite counter tops, solid wood furniture. It was recently renovated and has new kitchen appliances: refrigerator, stove, dishwasher and washing machine (no dryer but a line in the backyard). There are big front and back windows, an all glass door to the back garden and a skylight in the entry hallway ie, pretty good light for a fully attached building. The apartment is fully furnished in high quality : a full sized double bed with a brand new mattress (March 2020), night tables and lamps, wood dresser & wood wardrobes, lovely 1940s original loveseat in the bedroom: gorgeous Amish dining table, original 1960s chairs and lots of cabinet space in the kitchen/dining area; a storage bench, futon couch, end tables and brass lamps, built-in wood cabinets including one made by my grandfather, flat screen tv and a coffee table in the living area. Also Bose speakers for plugging in your music.

There's plenty of space for your things: a big under-the-stairs closet fully empty, one bedroom wardrobe fully empty (the other has our stuff stored in it) and half of our bedroom dresser. The kitchen has all you need but also space for your food.

All kitchenware (pots, pans, dishes, glasses, everything you need to cook and eat well) and all linens (sheets, pillowcases, towels, washcloths, blankets) are included. These are good quality: no teflon, no polyester. There is a split system in-wall air conditioner and 3 big ceiling fans to keep you cool in the humid NYC summer There's steam heat which you control and plenty of good hot water and water pressure. All utilities, including some tv (local + blu-ray), landline phone and wifi are included.

The back garden has tomatoes and herbs and raspberries, azaleas, a fig tree, and grapes, etc. It needs TLC and it will reward you. Please do not rent this apartment if you cannot do basic maintenance of the garden such as turning on the water in summer! There is also a small bbq, a garden table and chairs for relaxing and watching the birds (We have a resident flame-red cardinal). In the small shared front garden, you can hang out on the steps Brooklyn style and you can lock your bicycle off the street.

The whole house is eco-friendly with 'green' insulation, low emission paints and varnishes used throughout, water filtered on entry to the house, all non-toxic cleaning and laundry, no smoking or pets ever in the house, mostly vegetarian cooking, and a regular eco-bug treatment to keep insects away.

Guest access
The front garden is shared with 2 other people. The front door opens into a hallway shared with the same 2 people. Then you enter your fully private apartment: private bedroom, living room, kitchen and bathroom. And a private back garden.

Other things to note
Please understand that you are renting our apartment; it is not a generic space but our personal space and it contains furniture and other items that are important to us. We expect you to respect our home and to leave it in good condition. We in turn leave plenty of space for your belongings and, most importantly now, we give our full attention to safety: the apartment will be empty for at least 3 days before your arrival and it will have been thoroughly cleaned with all surfaces disinfected.

listing_name: The Blue Room by Prospect Park
description:
Experience a different era before skyscrapers, smart phones and consumer culture took over. Take a quiet moment, unwind and step back in time.....

Immerse yourself into the charm and warmth of my home. From the original kitchen pantry closets to the hand sculpted oak decorative carvings, you will have a comfortable place to spend the night after days spent out exploring this magnificent city.

Inspired by history suited for today. The ultimate NYC experience starts here.

The space
Built in 1902, the house was originally designed as respite to families fleeing the hustle and bustle of the City. Enjoy a private, bright, and spacious bedroom with access to a private bathroom located on the third-floor of a Brooklyn rowhouse, rich in history and original wood details. The kitchen is fully equipped with stainless steel appliances and access to the backyard. There are hardwood floors and high ceiling throughout the home.

Your room is fully equipped with a coffee & tea maker, extra linens, and
a full sized bed. It includes a writing desk and chair, three-drawer chest, and a closet, and is conveniently located next to a modern and newly renovated bathroom.

Guest access
The house is large and guests have access to the large eat-in kitchen, the parlor room and family rooms on the second floor, and their bedroom and private full guest bathroom on the third floor.

You also have access to the outdoor seating area for smoking.

During your stay
My level of interaction depends on what guests are looking for--if you want a quiet private experience or if you're looking to interact, I'm pretty flexible and can accommodate anyone!

I'm around for questions and guidance if guests need but the house is large and private enough that they can have their own space and experience.

Other things to note
A couple things to know before you book.....

My overweight chihuahua barks when he meets you which is his way of asking for attention. He will never bite or harm you:)

description:

Near Beautiful Prospect Park and Barclays Center; close to Bed-Stuy, Willamsburg, Ft Greene, and Park Slope.
Very Convenient location! near all the subways and Eastern Parkway. next door to (S) Shuttle.

Wide and long railroad apartment with 2 entrances, located on friendly street off Franklin Ave- the hip happenin spot!- full of cafes, bars, and restaurants! The apartment is situated on the S train line which is fairly quiet.

The space
Located in hip Pro-Cro Brooklyn; steps from the Brooklyn Museum, Botanical Gardens, and Prospect park..not to mention cozy & delicious cafes, bars, and bistros.(And a wonderful Yoga studio!). This is a private, beautiful, sun-drenched 3 Bedroom Large Apartment in Prospect Heights.

On the express 2,3, 4, 5 Franklin stop in Brooklyn, and the Franklin Ave Shuttle. There is a small shuttle train next to the building, so if you are a light sleeper, keep in mind that there is some noise.

Kitchen: We ask that you do not bring meat or bacon into our home. We are vegetarian!
Help yourself to hot water and tea!
Kitchen and cooking use is available - upon advance ONLY request. For a small fee, We will set it up desginated plates, bowls, silverware and a pot and pan etc for you specifically. other closed off cabinets and kitchen appliances are off limits. If you are looking for an Airbnb where you can cook and bake meals elaborate meals, this is not the right place for you!
The kitchen is not set up for guests: that means there are personal items out and guests are kindly asked not to use the non guest kitchen materials.
Essentially, Under extenuating circumstances, with advance notice, the kitchen can be made available with an added fee.

You may eat meals in our spacious serene dining room. please use placemats or a tablecloth on the tables.
We both love art and culture, and our apartment is the opposite of sterile, the walls are dressed in Rothko, Klimt, & Chaggall; counters coupled with handmade jars & mugs; and the shelves lined with diverse and interesting books.

This apartment is a private home, and there are personal items out. Please keep this in mind when you are booking the apartment, as it is a lived in comfy home. (it is not a bare empty space!)

My room has a queen size bed, desk, great sound system, and 3 sunny windows. There is storage space in the wardrobe, and handcrafted dresser, and plenty of floor space.
The second bedroom has a full sized bed, and the room adjoins a study with a desk.

listing_name: Chic, quiet 1BR with patio (steps from the subway)
description:
The space
Huge chicly decorated 1 bedroom, 1 bathroom with large private garden/patio and eat-in kitchen on one of the most charming blocks in Brooklyn.

This art filled floor of a brownstone features a large bedroom with its own bathroom, a large living room with a flat screen TV with a Roku (with Netflix and Amazon Prime for you to use) and a large well equipped eat-in kitchen. Out the back door of the kitchen is the private sunfilled patio/back yard. Both bedroom and living room have ceiling fans and the bedroom has an air conditioner.

The bedroom has a comfortable queen bed as well as a cozy reading chair. The living room couch folds out to a full bed. All linens and towels are provided.

The apartment is right on the border between Prospect Heights and Crown Heights and a block away from the fabulous Franklin Ave strip which features such awesome restaurants as Mayfield, Barbinco and Dutch Boy Burger as well as some of the best bars around. It is also one block away from the Brooklyn Botanical Gardens and the spectacular Brooklyn Museum as well as a short walking distance from Prospect Park, the Brooklyn Children's Museum and the bustling scene on Vanderbilt Ave.

It's a 3 minute walk to the 2/3 and 4/5 express trains at Franklin Ave. It's just one stop to the new Barclay's Center on the 4/5 and a quick 30-40 minute trip to Time's Square on the 2/3.

Guest access
Guests stay in their own private apartment which is a full floor of the brownstone. They also have access to a private back yard/patio.

Large sunny bedroom in a 3 bedroom old RAILROAD style apartment on the 4th floor (top floor walk up).

description:
The space
I am renting my lovely one bedroom apartment that is located in a waterfront property with nice views over Long Island Sound and direct water access.

The place is very conveniently located in City Island, a historic place famous for its seafood and sailing traditions. It is a very safe place, and everything is in walking distance, bars, coffee shops, grocery stores, art stores, banks, post office, and some of the best seafood restaurants of NYC within 5 minutes walk. It is a great place to relax if you need to work in NYC, and still would like to feel on your own island!
For commuting to NYC, you need to take B
X29 bus to Pelham Bay Park subway station, and then take the 6 line Express to Manhattan. It will take you about 45 minutes to 1 hour to get to midtown. There is also an express bus from City Island to Manhattan, as an alternative for commuting. There is street parking on the island, and you can park your bicycle on the property.

If you love the city, and do not want to forgo a nice outdoor life, my place cannot get any better for you, biking or hiking to NYC's largest park (Pelham Bay Park), fishing, boating, horseback riding, jogging. For beach lovers, the Orchard Beach is also in fair walking distance.

Guest access
The house has a large shared garden with direct access to the beach, you will not believe you are in NYC! You can BBQ and in summer use Canoe and Kayak.

Other things to note
The apartment is located in the second floor of the main house

description:

Nice linens, eco cleaning supplies, fancy Japanese water filter and zen like decor including a library of books on the healing arts. Great park on the corner, farmers market, Brooklyn flea, best restaurants, independent shops & businesses, lovely neighbors and Apple TV/projector for home entertainment

The space
Hello!

I have a one bedroom apartment that is available to rent in the heart of Ft Greene just off of Fort Greene Park, one of Brooklyn's best neighborhoods. Both rooms are fully furnished plus towels and sheets. Every room has hardwood floors, big windows, with great light.
Both rooms have a queen sized bed with Kangen water machine, Seally Posturepedic mattress, biomat, magazines, teas, subway maps and other helpful guides.

Location:
The apartment is in historic Fort Greene, Brooklyn, on the corner of Fort Greene Park, a very safe and charming neighborhood. listing_name: Large room in Beautiful communal house
description:
This is a large spacious apartment over 2 floors. The room is large and spacious and has a wall AC and built in closets. It also has a black board wall where you can write your thoughts or your plans. The house itself would be shared with 2 permanent roommates and then another Airbnb room which hosts an array of interesting and friendly people. This is a place away from home which will make you feel at home

The space
Hi Guys,

We have a room in our beautiful brownstone in Bushwick. The house has a great vibe and all of us are warm and welcoming. We are all in out mid to late 20's, very friendly and gay friendly. In addition to us there is also 2 amazingly chilled out cats.

You will have you own room with a huge closet and share 1 and a half bathrooms with the rest of the house. The house is big and spacious with a great outdoor area, so we wont be living on top of each other like other New York places.

Last but not least. I am an English teacher and I will be more that happy to help you with your English.

Look forward to hearing from you

listing_name: Condo Living -Perfect for Long Stays- Mins to NYC
description:
Your dates are open on our perpetually updated calendar, so go ahead and secure your spot – our space is ready and waiting for you. Any questions? Shoot us a message!

Step into convenience with our slick keyless entry system. Just a swift 5-minute ride from LaGuardia Airport, our place is a haven for the savvy traveler. Access the train station via a relaxed 15-minute stroll or hop on the bus for a quick 5-minute journey.

The space
Sleep like royalty with a plush queen-size bed, a lounge-ready full-size sofa bed, and an extra queen-size air bed for those who desire the royal treatment. Our prime location, a stone's throw from Manhattan, the legendary Arthur Ashe Stadium, CitiField, and the sprawling Flushing Meadows Park, caters to both work and play.

Seamless living is our mantra with the subway, bus stops, Queens Center Mall, and major highways all within arm's reach. Dive into the diverse culinary scene, enjoy parks, and more. Laundry day? No problem – washer and dryer facilities are included.

Embark on a swift 30-minute adventure to Manhattan or opt for a 15-minute cab ride ($30-$45). Wander through the vibrant streets of Little India, uncover hidden gems in local parks, and indulge in a variety of restaurants.

Other things to note
Quick tip: pls have a complete profile for a speedy process. Sorry, chain smokers NOT welcome. We're eager to host you – prepare for an unforgettable stay!

We welcome thoughtful, respectful guests and strive to offer a smooth, enjoyable stay. Please note that clear communication and courtesy are expected. Inquiries or behavior that are evasive, dismissive, or discourteous may result in declined bookings.

description:
Warm and Cozy newly renovated bedroom in a family home. Grab the bus on the corner to downtown Flushing and try the best dumplings in all New York. Jump on the 7 train to Time Square. Don't spend all your money on a hotel.

The space
This room is 10x12 and located on the second floor of a two family home. My bedroom is above yours on the third floor. It has a new semi firm ikea full size mattress that sleeps two average sized people (double or full size bed). There is a television with a Chrome Cast in your room with On Demand programming available. The bathroom and kitchen are shared spaces.

Come visit the biggest China town in all New York!! 12 min walk from the 7 train and downtown Flushing, or, just catch the bus on the corner. Excellent Dim Sum, Hot Pot, Korean BBQ, and Indian food. I can help you find your way around NY. Come stay with me and my family. :)

No pets, no babies/toddlers, no additional guests, no drugs and no smoking inside the room please. We prefer guests who keep their voices down after 9 pm. I have a cat, so not a good match for those of you with bad allergies. I will supply you with bath towels, hair towels, wash rags, body wash, shampoo, conditioner, hairdryer, slippers, and an umbrella. you bring the rest. :)

Me? I'm just a happy housewife with 2 kids and 1 cat. I enjoy playing with my two kids, dancing in the kitchen, volunteering at school, and interacting with my guests. Let me make you feel welcomed.

Guest access
Guests have access to Wifi, shared kitchen, and shared bathroom. During warmer months, guests can relax in the front yard or backyard. We have toiletries and guest towels, as well as house slippers for our guests to use.

During your stay
I love interacting with my guests!!!

Other things to note
Please note, this is not a hostel, it is not a motel, or a hotel, it is my home. Please be conscious that anything you break, I have to pay for. All the extra towels you use, I have to get laundered, lastly if you decide to cook very strong food, the odor will linger in my house for days or longer. Any mess you make, I have to clean by myself. Please be considerate to me and my family.

listing_name: Large Full-Size 1br Prime East Village Apartment
description:
My spacious (600ft) one-bedroom apartment is in the heart of East Village with full living room and kitchen. St. Marks is right around the corner, walking distance to Soho, Union Square, West Village, Lower East Side, Nolita, and Chinatown.

The space
This location, size of the apartment and the price is truly unbeatable. The apartment is a spacious (600ft) furnished one-bedroom with a living room, kitchen AND a separate study area. The bedroom is very large and the bathroom is inside of the bedroom.

Guest access
You are welcome to the whole apartment and feel free to use cooking utensils if you cook. However, please wash the dishes before you leave. Do not move around bulk furniture or shelf pieces.

Please note that this is my home, not a hotel. There are shower towels but not much of other personal items offered. I only rent my apartment when I'm traveling. Please make yourself comfortable at home but also treat it as if it is your friend's place. Please leave it as how you would like your friend to come back home to. Thank you.

Other things to note
Please note that there's no TV in the apartment. There's also not a sofa but a ergonomic chair and some stools. It's a little bare-boned but very convenient, clean and spacious. It's NYC, get out and explore!

Please also note that I’m renting this apartment at a price lower than what I pay and it’s already been discounted for monthly and multi-month renting. This already is the best deal you can find in the neighborhood for a full one-bedroom apartment, you are paying less rent than long-term lease. So unfortunately I can not go any lower on the price. Thank you.

listing_name: Stunning Designer Loft in the heart of Chelsea
description:
This stylishly decorated loft is located in the heart of Chelsea! With its tall ceilings and large living room, it will guarantee a luxurious stay in New York! It has a large bedroom with an ensuite bathroom with both a tropical shower and bathtub, a fully equipped kitchen, a large dining table, a second bathroom with shower and even an electrical piano for music lovers!
It is one block away from the subway station and only 10 blocks away from the Empire State Building!

The space
STUNNING DESIGNER LOFT IN CHELSEA

This elegant and modern Chelsea loft was originally built in the late 1800s and completely renovated in 2006. It features 14 ft ceilings, ebonized hardwood floors, stunning marble bathrooms, designer furniture and 6 huge windows with south and west exposures that keep the open kitchen, living and dining areas well-lit throughout the day.

Private and quiet… Enjoy cooking in the fully equipped kitchen and its limestone countertops. Sleep soundly on a Westin Heavenly mattress with a 1000-thread count bedsheets in the master bedroom and its ensuite bathroom with a huge bathtub.
Spend your time in NYC in the heart of Chelsea and close to everywhere you’d want to be in downtown Manhattan. Public transportation is readily available at your doorstep and major subways are less than 2 blocks away. It really is the perfect location!

LOFT FEATURES

- 1400 sq ft designer loft with heating and central air
- Large master bedroom with Westin Heavenly mattress
- Additional guests / children accommodated on Ligne Roset couch/bed
- 2 bathrooms (1 with rain shower and huge bathtub and 1 with shower)
- 55 inch HDTV with blu ray, cable and apple tv in the living room and 32 inch HDTV with DVR and cable in the master bedroom
- High-speed internet WiFi in all rooms
- Fully furnished with all houseware, linens and towels
- Washer/dryer
- Electric piano
- Fully stocked kitchen with refrigerator, gas stove, oven, dishwasher, microwave, blender, water kettle, silverware and pots and pans

LOCATION

- 4 blocks to Madison Square Park and the iconic Flatiron building
- 10 min walk to Chelsea Market
- 4 blocks from Eataly
- A few blocks away from night life in the Meatpacking district
- Close to West Village, Soho and 5th avenue shopping
- Restaurant recommendations available upon request
- Subway within a block
