description:
The space
This 3 bedroom apartment in Bed-Stuy Brooklyn (the neighborhood that brought you Jay-Z, Lil' Kim, The Notorious B.I.G., Big Daddy Kane, Mos Def, and was home to the first Black American Major League Baseball player, the legendary Jackie Robinson), offers spacious, modern living in a historically rich and diverse but tranquil neighborhood packed with beautiful brownstones on tree lined streets. 30 mins to financial district/Statue of Liberty Ferry, 45 mins to mid-town/Times Sq. Travelers can get here from JFK via the A Train Utica stop ($7) or the B15 Bus ($2.50) which stops at our corner. (The bus may take longer)

Reviews: Please don't be confused by the reviews here on Air BnB. There are reviews about our current apartment and our PREVIOUS apartment. That previous apartment is no longer available but it should give you an idea of the level of service we offer and our longstanding relationship with Air BnB. We take pride in providing the best lodging for our guests.

2025 Note: We have recently finished landscaping The large sunbathing/garden space. We are in the process of accessorizing the apartment so it's a bit Spartan at the moment but still cozy and immaculate. If you are in need of any amenities that we have overlooked, please just let us know. We live right upstairs and will do our best to accommodate any and all reasonable requests in a reasonable amount of time.

Note 2: As always when traveling on Holiday, be aware of your surroundings. Search on the internet about the neighborhood you're considering! Brooklyn is a wonderful place full of some amazingly nice people. But it is not Disneyland. We will advise you upon arrival about any safety concerns or areas you should avoid in Brooklyn. (Other boroughs...we can't help too much there.) But this is New York city...try not to look like a tourist...ask us questions and use basic common sense that you would use at home or in any other urban area.

This bright, spacious, and tranquil apartment is located in the heart of Williamsburg, Brooklyn's trendiest neighborhood. Only four blocks from the Domino Park waterfront (iconic old sugar factory), the neighborhood is bustling with the greatest shopping, restaurants, coffee shops, bars, and cinemas that NY has to offer. It is close to the L, G, J, M, Z trains which take you anywhere in Brooklyn, Manhattan, or Queens very conveniently. This apartment is in the best location & still quiet.

The space
Amazing, one-of-a-kind, 718 sq.ft. apartment with exposed brick in the heart of Williamsburg.

Very spacious, creative space. Cozy and warm, facing south and gets a lot of sunshine!

2 flight walk-up in a clean BRAND NEW building with up to date appliances and a laundry room in the basement.

1 spacious & very bright bedroom w/ therapeutic queen-sized mattress, desk, dresser, and large closet w/ hangers.

21'x9' living room
separate kitchen including all the essentials w/ island counter/bar, 2 bar stools
large dining table, 2 chairs
electric outlets all throughout the apt
Central air+heat

1 bathroom w/ shower+tub+amenities
towels+clean bed-sheets provided

description:
This clean, sunny studio cottage is a perfect quiet hideaway for a single person or couple looking to explore NYC. Situated behind an 1850s wood-frame house, guests enjoy a separate entrance and ultimate privacy in historic Fort Greene, Brooklyn.

The space
This clean, sunny studio cottage is the perfect quiet hideaway for a single person or couple looking to spend some time exploring New York City. Situated behind an 1850s wood-frame house, guests enjoy their own entrance and ultimate privacy (but we are close by and always happy to help with any questions). Amenities include one full-sized bed, skylight, AC/heat, bathroom/shower with plenty of fresh linens, small kitchen with cooktop, refrigerator, and toaster oven (no full oven), free Wi-Fi, and coffee freshly roasted (by Allon, at our cafe) to enjoy during your stay :).

Guest access
This space is completely detached from our home. You enter through a locked gate alongside the house to the backyard where you enter the cottage through its own entrance. This affords lots of privacy, and many of our guests comment on how quiet and private the space is. You are also welcome to sit in the sun and enjoy our backyard garden, right outside your door.

Other things to note

description:
The space
Hello, and thank you for viewing my page.

I have a large airy very sunny bedroom to rent which is located 1 1/2 blocks from Central Park and 2 blocks from Riverside. Shoes are not worn in the house, slippers are provided.The bed is full/queen with 3 windows in the room and very lovely sheets. The bathroom is shared and towels etc are provided. I am a gym goer, so I shower there daily. The kitchen is charming with new appliances and old world detail.You may help yourself to a french press cafe in the morning with a ytThe apartment is on the top floor (9th) with elevator. One flight up to the roof, the view is east side across the park and down toward midtown Manhattan. The apartment is quiet.

LOCATION

The apartment is 1 1/2 blocks from Central Park. Just walk or take the Cross Town bus across the park and you will be at the Met Museum on 5th Avenue. Museum Mile - Guggenheim,Frick,Nueve Gallerie. If you are a runner, conveniently located to the Reservoir path for running. National History is a 2 blocks away as well as The New York Historical Museum. Restaurants and bars within walking distance on the avenues - Columbus and Amsterdam. A ten minute walk and you are at Lincoln Center. Subways the A,B,C lines 2 blocks and the 1,2,3, 3 blocks. You can get anywhere in NYC from here - 10 min. from Times Square and 20 from the 9/11 museum. Many fabulous shops are also in this area. If you want,there are prepared food at westsider's favorites, Fairway, Trader's Joe's and Zabars. Also, the apartment is just a 1/2 block away from Cafe Lalo where \You Got Mail\" was filmed. listing_name: Chic Brownstone Apt. with Outdoor Patio
description:
Beautiful Private Suite, Legal Airbnb, in modern Two Bedroom Garden Apartment in a Morris historic district owner occupied Harlem Brownstone with an outdoor patio and garden, modern kitchen, WIFI/Cable TV, Central AC, washer/dryer. Check in 4pm, checkout 10am. Just two blocks to 125th St. subways, buses on the corner. Close to Whole Foods, fashion shopping, Columbia University, CCNY, SUNY graduate center, Red Rooster, Apollo Theater, Museum Mile and a 10 minute walk to Central Park.

The space
Our historic brownstone's first floor garden apartment has a separate entrance and is newly renovated with one bedroom with a queen size bed, with dressers and closets. You look out into a garden with a beautiful Mulberry tree that ensures privacy and calm in the bustle of the city. Sit outside on the patio and enjoy the garden and grill. A spacious living room with modern sleeper couch and a dining area provide comfortable communal space.
All modern conveniences: Washer/Dryer, Dishwasher, Granite counter tops, stainless steel appliances, gas stove with grill, oven, espresso coffee machine, microwave oven, Exposed Brick, Central A/C, Radiant heat, Patio on a quiet residential street. Wireless Internet. Cable TV. High ceilings. Fully furnished with cotton linens and all kitchenware.

Guest access
Entrance is on the ground floor secured by an iron gate and through a shared vestibule. Garden access is through rear bedroom.

During your stay
Host is available at all times and follows NYC law. You are always welcome to ask any questions. Host phone numbers and email are also posted on the welcome letter in the apartment.

Other things to note
Our apartment uses real wooden doors and moldings. Please note our welcome letter on the refrigerator for basic information and house rules.

listing_name: Large Private Bedroom in Chic Apartment Share!
description:
Private Bedroom in Shared Pre-War 1-bedroom Apartment, with access to the office, kitchen and bathroom. It's on Super Quiet Street yet in the heart of NYC's fantastic Upper West Side! Steps from Riverside Park, restaurants, drug stores, the Subway and five minute walk to always-open supermarket. This charming home-away-from-home has easy access to all the sights in the city.

The space
Your bedroom is extremely quiet in this nicely appointed and very comfortable apartment on the 4th floor of a Pre-War elevator building. The bedroom has a flat screen TV, a very comfortable queen size bed with a 4 inch memory foam topper and 2 large windows on the courtyard. This is a very safe and quiet building in a very safe and quiet neighborhood. Equipped with a video intercom to the lobby.

Guest access
Use the fast WiFi, large flat screen TV (comes with streaming service with hundreds of channels) and the well-equipped cook's kitchen with dishwasher, espresso maker, likely everything you could need. Laundry facilities in the basement. This is a musicians apartment with a grand piano. If you play, please feel free to keep it company.

During your stay
Upon your arrival I'll meet you to exchange keys, show you around the apartment and answer any questions you may have at that time. After that, I am glad to be available to answer any questions or help with any concerns that may arise during your stay.

Other things to note
This is a perfect spot for one or two visitors to NYC.

listing_name: Less than a minute from the Metro
description:
Come and enjoy our spacious house in Prospect Lefferts Garden. Affordable accommodation, just half a block to the Manhattan-bound subway. Convenient, comfortable, safe, no-frills, large space. The rooms are on the second floor with access via a staircase. The host occupies another room not shown in the photos in the apartment and shares some area.

If your stay is less than 30 days, the host occupies a separate room on the same floor, and shares living room and bathroom.

The space
One large bedroom with queen size bed and windows facing the street side, plus one attached, smaller room with a double bed. Shared kitchen, bathroom, and dining room.

Guest access
Guests will be provided with their own door code.

During your stay
We are happy to answer any questions before you come and during your stay. We respect your privacy but will be available if you ever need us.

Other things to note
About locking doors:

Please lock the red main front door (the top) every time you leave the house.
Please do not lock the black, inside door.

House Rules

Please remove your shoes when inside the apartment. We provide disposable slippers.

Check-in time is after 3:00PM. Check-out time is before 11:00AM.

If anything is broken or damaged, please let us know. We consider not to charge for any accidental damage if you mention before you leave.

Please turn off the lights and air conditioners when you leave the house.

Please separate household garbage from recycling (boxes, paper, plastic, and glass).

Please keep the apartment in good condition. There is a cleaning charge if you leave an excessive mess.

No smoking in the house including incense.

Luggage:

Our check out time is 11:00AM, however if someone wants to stay longer or keep luggage for a few hours we will try to help whenever possible.

listing_name: Artsy & Cozy. 2 stops from Manhattan! W/D in unit.
description:
Clean, cozy, and artsy 1 bedroom charming apartment on the 5th floor of a secure elevator building. Adorable balcony, in-unit washer/dryer, stainless steel appliances, 2 train stops to the city. Close to 5 train lines. J,M,Z,L,G. Near Brooklyn Harvest Market, Walgreens, coffee spots, thrift stores, bars and restaurants. Park nearby.

The space
The bedroom has 3 pocket doors that section off the bedroom from the living room. There is a lot of light in both the bedroom and living room when you want to open the curtains and enjoy the large bay window or keep it closed for privacy. Blackout shades in the bedroom for a better night's sleep. Queen tempurpedic bed.

Beautifully appointed bathroom with a bidet. Subway tiled and stone walls/flooring. Huge wrap around mirror. Take a nice hot shower or drift away in the bathtub (complete with a dimmed lighting for when you want to mellow out.)

The open kitchen is fully stocked with a microwave, oven, refrigerator and everything you need to make your own meals.

There is also a lovely outdoor space just off of the bedroom. Sit, enjoy your coffee and read the paper.

Steps away from Brooklyn Harvest market, Walgreens, Police and fire station. Williamsburg Pizza, Brooklyn Ball Factory coffee.

*Please note a dog normally lives in the apartment. The cleaning person does a good job of removing excess hair. Please let me know if there are any issues.

Guest access
You have the whole apartment to yourself.

Other things to note
Utilities are incurred if you stay 30 days or longer. You will be responsible for gas/electric. Free Wi-Fi. Inquire within if looking for a place for 3-week stays or less, will work with you. ;)

description:
This is a 3 bedroom historical brownstone. You will have 2 bedrooms. I live on the other.

Featuring high ceilings, wooden floors, and windows looking to the trees.

The house has a relaxing back deck. Two bathrooms.

It’s near restaurants and coffee places.

Close to the A-C line and also to the J line. 20-30 minutes to Manhattan in the subway.

I’m a filmmaker and I live in this duplex house. But I work long hrs.

The space
Welcome to our charming Brownstone, nestled in a quiet and historic neighborhood that exudes timeless charm. Your stay includes a big room featuring a luxurious king-size bed with a Sleep Number mattress, allowing you to adjust the firmness to your preference for each side of the bed. This spacious room can accommodate up to three guests, perfect for couples or small families, and we can provide an extra bed upon request.

The second bedroom offers a cozy twin-size bed, ideal for additional guests or children, with a desk and TV for a workstation. Our home is designed to ensure you have a restful and enjoyable stay.

You'll find a variety of delightful restaurants, coffee shops, and grocery stores just a short walk away.

Parking is hassle-free with easy street parking available. Plus, our location offers excellent connectivity to Manhattan with two subway lines nearby. The J train at Halsey stop is just a 6-minute walk, and the C train at Howard stop is an 8-minute walk, allowing you to reach downtown Manhattan in just 25-30 minutes.

Our neighborhood is known for its beautiful, tree-lined streets and historical brownstones. You’ll have everything you need for an unforgettable New York experience.

Relax on our spacious deck overlooking the lush backyard, perfect for unwinding after a day of exploring the city.

Traveling from JFK Airport is a breeze with the AirTrain to the subway, bringing you straight to our doorstep. La Guardia is also close by in a taxi.

Book your stay with us and immerse yourself in the quintessential Brooklyn experience, blending historic charm with modern comfort.

During your stay
I live in the house but I assure you full privacy. There are two bathrooms one on each floor so I usually use the downstairs bathroom during the day.
